posts.author: ImperatorFishrat
posts.body: Logged in just to say Goodbye to a couple of people which I would have done during the last card of the year had Greg not suspended me (thus breaking the agreement we had made which he still seemingly has chosen not to honor). It's about what I have come to expect from such a ridiculously catty, small-minded, vindictive wreck of a man.Although I'm probably just casting pearls before swine, I'd like to clarify that Greg suspended me for making statements about Muslims--specifically to the effect that I stated I didn't particularly care how many were killed in the Middle East. There is no Muslim race. Islam is not a race any more than scientology is a race. Greg is a scurrilous sperg who cannot grasp the distinction and now I find him deliberately misrepresenting what transpired and led to my suspension. Bravo, detective. And go **** yourself.Moving on, I've had a really good time talking **** with you guys. Time enjoyed wasting isn't exactly wasted time. Regardless, this is the last Tapology post I will ever make.Godspeed, Brevrin.A forest is not a field of trees.

posts.author: NickySwaz
posts.body: Okay so the Jones story, my cousin Luke, very smart kid went to Cornell not too far from where Jon grew up, Cornell has a great wrestling program and Jon was up there to put in some work on the mats, or so we though. My cousins frat throws a party, and guess who shows up, Jon Bones Jones. It’s a college frat party so there are nefarious actions happening all over the place but the real story happens in a bedroom upstairs. My cousin and four other people are doing coke when Jon walks in and starts doing some with them, kids start to trickle out but not my cousin, eventually it’s him, Jon and like 3 lines left. Jon looks at my cousin dead in the eyes and goes ‘if i wanted to **** you right i could and there’s nothing you could do about it’ so my cousin did the smart thing and gave him the rest of the cocaine. Then they went back down stairs and kept partying. A month or two later Jon got popped and the DC rematch got canceled.

posts.body: @HippieI run the PPC (Pink Prediction Championship) fantasy games if you're interested! It consists of 3 events, 1 UFC event, one boxing event and one lesser known MMA event. The idea is that the winner would be the user with the best all-round combat sports knowledge. I've run 13 PPC events so far and I have just set up the PPC 14. I set them up when the old one ends. It’s free to enter but then again there are also no chips to be won but for what it’s worth, I’ll follow the winner (or winners in the event of a tie) of each PPC, along with a congratulations comment on their wall and an invite to the next PPC. 2nd and 3rd place (including any joint 2nd or 3rd places) will get a congratulations comment on their wall. The reason for this is that I don’t want anyone to want to join the game but not being able to afford to while also still having some kind of prize structure (although not much of one) but it is just a friendly competition after all! There is also a PPC group as well that has things such as a thread that documents all the PPC events and another thread that informs members of the group about new games as well. Here are the links if you or anyone else is interested:PPC 14 link: https://www.tapology.com/fantasy/32557PPC Group: https://www.tapology.com/groups/503 * Edited at 01.31.2021, 7:58 PM ET *

posts.author: Drmaxis2
posts.body: @HippieRandom thought at a game though, and feel free to tell me if it's **** or not. Participants pick a team of 5 fighters (no repeats so there would need to be a draft like they do in the prospect game) and it's somewhat like a combination of the Survivor game and Wagering League. The rule that's similar to the Survivor Game is that if any of the fighters on your team lose, you're eliminated so you don't want any particular weak links on your team. Conversely, when somebody on your team wins a fight you get 1 point, so part of the trick in doing well is picking fighters that are active enough to accrue you points over the time frame of the game (could be 1 month, 6 months, or 1 year) but aren't going to screw up your whole game by losing either. Most points OR last person with none of their team losing by the end of a the time frame (TBD) wins. Any thoughts on my idea are welcome. It kind of feels a little like fantasy football too, but with higher stakes on individual team members.

posts.author: Drmaxis2
posts.body: HippieIn theory it could be any org, but if it were purely my brain child I'd do only UFC since I just care about those fights more than others (it's also a huge common denominator for users of this site I'd imagine). I'm not sure about how it'd work in relation to "the" Draft Game on here because I don't believe I've ever seen it, but there'd be some time frame allocated for people to pick fighters, (lots would be drawn to determine the order of picking of course). Could do 3 points just to make the number totals look more impressive, but if it's the same value for getting a win for everybody then it wouldn't matter a ton. It'd also be possible to incorporate perfect picks into the scoring as well if we wanted to do that. BTW, I've just been spit-balling so far, and am not committing to running the game (but I wouldn't completely rule that possibility out either). Feel free to ask any more questions or make more suggestions, as I said, this was just an off the cuff idea I had and I'm not super stuck to any idea other than single eliminations for being wrong since I love that format. PS I'd probably call it, "The Weakest Link Game" or something.

posts.body: Oh, by the way Hippie I missed your suggestion in the heading of your post. I'd say if anything I'd do more fighters rather than less since fighters fight decently infrequently so there wouldn't be a ton of movement in the game most of the time. I think 5 is probably a nice number for not too many or too few though. If the trial run of this hypothetical game went on way longer than intended before a winner was chosen I'd then add more fighters to give everybody a higher likelihood of being eliminated. The only downside of having even 5 fighters is the drafting would probably take a while, and it'd be even worse with more picks. That being said, the only real downside to that is the set up.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @Drmaxis2You raise a good point about the time commitments to running a game like that. That's the same reason @poir420 wants to stop doing the Wagering League. In the case of this game, if everyone had 5 fighters, even if only 20 people played it, that's still 100 fighters to keep track of and you'd then have work out who's fighters have won and how many points they get, it sounds like a logistical nightmare to run. It sounds like a fun game to play but it might not be as fun for whoever is running it. For my PPC fantasy games, the only things I have to do is to keep track of the current game, update the group after every game (the group isn't part of the game itself, it's just a fun little side project plus I like to keep stats) and then set up a new one. Nice and easy so I still have fun doing it.If you wanted to do a small trial run for this hypothetical game then you could maybe do one for the next couple of weeks where a smallish group of players (maybe about 10 or so) pick maybe 2 or 3 fighters each for the next few UFC events and a few other events for the next couple of weeks and see how it goes. It's smaller scale but it would give you an idea of what running it on a larger scale might feel like. * Edited at 01.31.2021, 10:32 PM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @HippieI think the reason for that is because you have made so many picks that individual events are less likely to affect your %. What I mean is suppose a new user has 15 wins and 7 losses and then they do a Colosseum MMA card with 50 fights on it and they get them all wrong. That would tank their %. Equally, it would boost it if they got them all right. In your case however, if you did that and got them all wrong then those 50 losses would have less of an effect against your 15,695 wins than it would against that new user's 15. Equally, if you got them all right, those 50 wins would have less of an effect against your 7,099 losses. This is simply because those numbers are would only account to a small fraction of your total picks because you've made so many. You'd have to have either a consistently good or bad run of picks for it to affect the %. I probably haven't explained it well but maybe someone who's better at maths could explain it better. * Edited at 02.03.2021, 11:33 AM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @TARTARAIf this were about 4 years ago, I would've recommended it to one of my mates Andrew who used to BMX but he gave it up after breaking his leg badly in a crash in 2017. I was with him when it happened, there's a fairly steep hill just outside of London called Box Hill and he was riding around and I tagged along as well as even though I didn't ride, I always enjoy hanging out with him. Anyway, I was standing at the top (not the very top but a steep part nonetheless) and he started down but a few seconds later he lost control and his right leg got caught in the frame of the bike and the bike flipped, effectively snapping his leg. That was the most disgusting looking injury I've ever seen and he's got a metal plate in his leg now. He gave up on BMX after that.Like I said, we weren't right at the top but that's Box Hill just to give you an idea of the sort of decline he was dealing with. * Edited at 02.17.2021, 8:18 PM ET *

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Strange @ss SUV ad.https://keys.craigslist.org/ctd/d/north-east-2019-jeep-grand-cherokee/7279140605.htmlThis Jeep Grand Cherokee can be yours today!Price: $179,991.00Year: 2019Make: JeepModel: Grand CherokeeOdometer: 666 milesEngine: V8, HEMI, Supercharged, 6.2 LiterTransmission: Automatic, 8-SpdColor: Black How fitting it is that it’s just in time for Halloween, as we have dubbed this King of all demons, this ruler of all hellcats, to be named "Lucifer". Starting life as a brand new 2019 Jeep Grand Cherokee Trackhawk, with not more than 100 break-in miles, we ripped the heart out of this monster and began the transformation process. The Hellcat 2.4L supercharger just wasn't going to be a big enough set of lungs for our fire breathing demon. Instead, we ditched the factory S/C and opted for the Whipple 4.5L, then stacked that with 40 pounds of nitrous on the baddest custom 4 bottle setup ever made. ARP head studs hold together a completely custom built valvetrain, featuring MMX pushrods, springs, and rocker arms. A custom ground camshaft regulates the heinous amounts of air and fuel being forced into each cylinder. Fuel is delivered via Fore Innovations complete L4 triple pump fuel system. 1300 series Injector Dynamics fuel injectors allow for full custom E85 and Ignite 114 fuel dyno tunes courtesy of Dusterhoff Tuning. Unlike so many high horsepower builds out there, we also built the drivetrain and suspension, not just to handle the massive amount of power, but to keep it planted where you want it. Unimaginable amounts of torque and horsepower course through a Pro-torque torque converter, DSS front and rear driveshafts, G Force Outlaw Axles, BWoody differential braces, ARP wheel studs, and finally into the custom billet monoblock ANRKY wheels. Proper geometry and support is attained through Mopar lowering springs and a complete BWoody suspension upgrade- adjustable control arms, camber kit, and swaybar links. These components ensure that all four 12" wide Michelin Super Sports have the widest contact patch touching the road at all times. The attention to detail on this custom-built masterpiece of engineering is truly unmatched. Custom touches lurk around every corner. The build list is entirely too long to list here. This is without a doubt the sickest Trackhawk on the road. So what are the numbers? What did this thing put down to the ground? For comparison, the stock Trackhawk makes roughly 575 HP and 533 lb-ft of torque at all four wheels. Running only a 50 shot of nitrous, and using a mustang dyno, "Lucifer" delivers an earth-shattering 1,055HP and 1,136 LB-FT of torque at all 4 wheels. NFI Empire is currently offering this 2019 Jeep Grand Cherokee Trackhawk "Lucifer" with 666 miles for $225,666

Then one day when they come for you and you thought they were on your side, then you will understand what happens when you limit speech.This is my basic summary about limiting speach.First they came for the socialists, and I did not speak out— Because I was not a socialist.Then they came for the trade unionists, and I did not speak out— Because I was not a trade unionist.Then they came for the Jews, and I did not speak out— Because I was not a Jew.Then they came for me—and there was no one left to speak for me.

posts.body: @Duck it may have started as a joke to you, but I've never taken anything so seriously in my life! Seeing Woodley's name on that list awakened in me what my subconscious mind knew all along, his ass is objectively better than every single name above him, and I feel this way with every fiber of my being. Even now, to think that Dern, Zingano, Carano, Rousey, Herrig, Albu, Correia, and Tate are in front of him makes me SICK! You might even say I have a bug up my ass about it. But I knew I couldn't turn my back on this cause. I knew I could no longer assent to live in a world where he doesn't have the position he rightfully deserves. So, passionately I took up a cause with which I hope all can show some compassion toward. Assist me brothers, in amassing enough support to reassert the Chosen One as THE ambassador of this ranking as he so tacitly deserves! posts.author: Tieoken
posts.body: Base pay with incremental increases per win with the promotion.Health insurance during contract tenure and non-optional health insurance for 90 days after any bout regardless of if the promotion lets them go.Viewership pay for all main card fightersPPV points for all title belt holders and contenders (obviously the holders get more)Required to be offered 4 bouts per year where all offers must be within the fighters selected weight class and within +/- 5 positions of the fighters current ranking. Terms negotiable based on fighters discretion.Sponsorship opportunities for all athletes on their shorts from outside organizations. If these sponsors want their exposure they can negotiate with the fighter or satisfy themselves with the canvas or ads.Post-career development program (this is actually already in existence I just like to call attention to it, shoutout to Forest Griffin).

posts.body: I'm actually diagnosed OCD by my psychiatrist who I'm sure does something despite the fact it seems every session involves me turning up, talking about novels for an hour, paying him 300$ and then walking out feeling the same as I walked in. I dunno. The half-life of a psych paper is about 5 years. I think a lot of the data produced in that sector might be either extremely poor or outright fallacious. Does anyone truly good at science opt-in to that industry? I have doubts.I sold the bulk of my PLTR @ 31$ just as it started dropping from resistance but still hold some shares. It's an amazing company but still trading far too high above its value like basically all tech stocks ( AMD/Intel are buy right now IMO though). I'm not really trading at the moment. Last thing I actively shorted was RBLX and did OK after getting my cheeks clapped by capital gains.Basically just holding 3-5 yearers, ETF's, some penny and/dividend stocks, some ASX mining venture things ( Silica particularly, it's getting harder and harder to get in high grades). I'm not really making anything but I sleep a lot better not worrying whether ****'s going to exercise/I'm going to get ****d by decay etc. I'm actually down 2% overall in my portfolio currently but March just happened and I don't give a ****. It's always crab dip at this time of year unless you're cashing leaps or just playing the options/CFD casino generally.Been active the last week or on the site but won't be very active moving forward--nothing like my previous rate in any case. Made this account to purely pick UFC/Bellasnore only. And of course to ****post occasionally.

posts.body: @Miller, so I got a Psych degree concurrent with my Neuroscience degree. The reason I did this is because the psych degree was so easy to get that it was basically a free piece of paper. I think having someone to chat with who is impartial and educated in the fundamentals is always helpful. But the vast majority of psychologists (who don’t have an MD) are insecure shills. They are the collegiate version of the “high school bully who turns to the army and then wants be called a hero while sitting in a com booth and actually contributing nothing. But it gives them a vague sense of superiority by saying they are in the military.” Most psychologists do good work, but the data pumped by that community is so poorly controlled and designed around preconceptions that even the good Science is basically, “Hey, sleeping well decreases the rate of violent altercations and improves overall quality of life WHO WOULDVE GUESSED!!!”

posts.author: Millershades
posts.body: Ahhhh Yes, 'NAZI'; the equivalent of mostly-sunny-with-a-chance-of-possible-showers-clearing of every ********'s argument everywhere.Apart from the fact that he's a rampant ideologue, it's very likely the case that Erison detests me because I'm talented, and he isn't---whether that particular brand of talent is to his taste or not is irrelevant. I don't like brass but you won't hear me say Louis Armstrong sucks.Erison will never create anything anyone enjoys reading ( or will pay to read) because he thinks in cliché', and not having an interesting mind will only create acre after acre of contrived boring Art. That's why he's cross- examining scripts like an old woman with a biro doing a 'spot the difference' featured in a tabloid, and not writing them. No matter how many lattes he fetches, no matter how many blowjobs he delivers under desks, he's just not ever going to be the guy driving artistic direction. I think he senses that. It's on-brand for those who can't to hate.I'm not surprised he veered of from writing some fight breakdown (which isn't interesting to anyone because it's 100% self-evident information that everyone has already deduced themselves, thanks Poirot) to Kazoo about how he identified that this is an alt account. I've seen the chromosomally deficient child with fried onions in his hair triumphantly solve the maze on the paper mat at burger king before.Regardless of the acerbic drivel I post on Tap (which is just for fun and excess to what I keep) I am a proper writer. I am pedantic, demented, dreamy, elegant and profane. What I do certainly isn't for everyone and there's probably not much of a market for it. But I'm very good at, it's the pieces that come my way, and it comes from an authentic place. As a general rule the more complicated and demanding something is, fewer people like it. But you have to do you. And what I've been working on for some time is extremely complicated and no, it isn't a fantasy novel. It's unfortunate that I'll never be able to post it on this forum for obvious reasons.But even if I did, and it was good enough to knock Proust into a ****ed hat, it would still be denigrated by imbeciles like Erison who have yet to prove to anyone that they are capable of writing a Hallmark card. That's the world.Sorry for the long post, but I appreciate everyone who's glad I'm back even in a limited capacity. I was trying to lay low but Greg who is obviously a genius, and could have chastised me off-site very easily, thumbed me officially here. And then floodgates opened. I'll sum up by reiterating again that if you don't like what I type, block me. It's not hard, and if you don't do it you're obviously just spoiling for a fight, like Erison, who feels compelled to tell everyone he would like to fight me.Thanks.

posts.body: @Erison you misunderstand me; I have no interest in writing for Television. That isn't to say I don't enjoy some TV series, I do. But I also enjoy skittles. But is say, David Benioff, a writer of the same Caliber as Mark Danielewski? If my point is unclear, it's this; I would much rather write a very quality book that very few people read, but the few who do absolutely love, that makes me next to nothing, than write a screenplay for something with the Artistic merit of Buffy the Vampire Slayer and see my name roll down the credits at the end of the episode and make lots of money in the process.And I don't expect someone like you to be able to understand why that is.Edit: I doubt very much IMDB'ing 'Erison' will turn up much honestly-- especially since it's quite obvious you've never written a screenplay because nobody venturing real money would ( or ever will) pay you to write anything.I'm going to get off the merry-go-round now and put you ignore. posts.author: Gregory
posts.body: Many years ago we actually made like 100 tshirts and put a link up to buy them. Not nearly as many people bought them as the number of people who SAID they would buy one!! So we got stuck with a lot of extra shirts which took time to slowly use up.Also the time and cost of packing up and mailing each shirt was a real pain in the ass! Folding up the shirt, typing in the shipping label information, printing it, taping the label on, etc. Then going to the post office or wherever. The whole thing was insane. For the few dollars in "profit" on each shirt, we probably would have been better off doing a ride as an Uber driver, in the same amount of time.Also I wasn't happy with the quality of the shirts and the printing. So there's some trial and error needed to get a satisfactory product.Probably the way to do it is to use some kind of dropshipping service that does the shipping/etc for you. Maybe some day we'll get it all figured out.

posts.author: ImperatorFishrat
posts.body: Bugger it. I'll break contract and risk a ban because I don't believe what is happening here is fair to Duck. And I consider the Duck my friend. It's wrong. Nobody should be tarred and feathered for making a personal health choice. I've decided not to take the vaccine due to an underlying medical condition (two actually) that make me susceptible to blood clots. It has nothing to do with conspiracies and yes, I 100% believe COVID-19 is real and isn't a hoax.Damien, it's your body and your choice. Over 99% of people who contract COVID survive it, and when co-morbidity is excluded from the Data the death count shrinks by orders of magnitude. You get to decide what your own level of risk tolerance is. Not a Celebrity. Not a Software developer. You. Nobody else. It isn't a moral quandary, and it has nothing to do with morality whatsoever. The pomposity and self-righteousness of people who genuinely feel morally superior to those who can't/choose not to get vaccinated is staggering. These same people, every day, buy useless crap/drink alcohol/ frivolously spend money on junk food knowing that that money could be sent to the third world and save a child's life. Getting a vaccine does not make you a morally superior being any more than wearing a bicycle helmet or wearing a seatbelt or taking out an insurance policy does. It's about you, not others. You're simply exercising agency. Spare me.Let's break it down logically: If the vaccine works it doesn't matter whether you take it or not because you can't infect those vaccinated people. The only people you can infect are people who have chosen not to be vaccinated, which makes it a push. If the Vaccines don't work, then there's also not much point in taking them anyway. I'm not saying they don't work. Probably it's the case they are like Flu shots and will require boosters which means they are an effective mitigation tool against the virus. Regardless, it's almost certainly the case that it's here to stay, and it is absolutely the case that mutations will continue to emerge when ostensibly everyone in society is vaccinated/has immunity. * Edited at 04.18.2021, 5:58 PM ET *

posts.author: Gregory
posts.body: @xconchris About your question on when do we list something different than what the commission or the promotion says, and when might we have an asterisk:We have a number of different guidelines we use, but also things are looked at on a case by case basis. For this example with the Haim Gozali "3 second" finish, there is no commission or regulator. This was not a well-run event. A 3 second finish would have put the fight as the fastest submission ever, on Tapology's list of fastest submissions. So in this case it's a new rule / new guideline for a special circumstance. You don't get to top our fastest submissions in the history of MMA list when your organization is completely sketchy and you didn't even start the timer properly.ALL THAT SAID, while the opponent tapped out VERY early and it was sketchy, there are plausible explanations for it. The opponent could have been a last-minute replacement who was terrified of having his knee ligaments ripped to pieces. I don't know what health insurance and knee surgery options look like in that part of rural Ukraine, but suffice to say they might not be world-class. And so was the fight thrown? Or was the opponent just overwhelmed and scared at what he knew was about to happen? We don't know, and so it's inconclusive. That's why we've let the result stand as-is for now. If other evidence emerges we can look at it. * Edited at 06.26.2021, 12:19 PM ET *

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: @Greg I have read what you wrote carefully and I still have to disagree. At what point does Tapology go from a recorder of fight records to a interpretor of results. This is a grey area that opens a Pandora's box that you won't be able to close. It's a line that which you cross, that the line will move. Multiple fights will eventually have different results from Tapology, Sherdog, MMA underground, Wikipedia and boxrec.Right now your views on guidelines and case to case basis for fights will change in a few years. Just like our views on everything changes over time. Eventually your successor will have different views and opinions as well. This is why opinion and personal bias has no place in being a recorder of fights. Especially when there's no definitive proof. Much like the angry mob came for Abdul Rasak Allhasan and he was found innocent. We can say this sketchy promotion most likely with absolute certainly engaged in some shady ****..but there's also a chance the opponent laid a egg, panicked for his life and tapped before the pressure was applied. This is why notation rather then official change for a record is my recommendation for results.At this point the goal post will be moved in the future for bad judging.Once we start making our own results we are no longer a fight result recorder but we deem ourselves the unofficial commission * Edited at 06.26.2021, 2:13 PM ET *

posts.author: Gregory
posts.body: @xcon while you are correct in bringing up the hard and challenging decisions that might need to be made, you are also over-complicating what is actually very simple. If a random promoter in another part of the world says "Hey look at this world record for the fastest submission EVER!" ... and we watch the video and that's FALSE, we are not going to post it as a world record. That's just plain stupid. So we timed it and recorded the correct time. End of story. * Edited at 06.26.2021, 2:39 PM ET *

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: The issue isn't bad judging. The issue is that the Athletic commissions are political bureaucracy's that put judges based on who they know rather then what they know. They refuse to punish, discipline and remove and allow poor judges to continue because of politics which should never be allowed to be in the same conversation as sports. I stated before that New Jersey is probably the best commission in my eyes. Very progressive. Puts athletes like Ricardo Almeida as judges, and most importantly.As anyone in a position not power, doesn't dig a hole, sink their heels in and defend it's position to the death. They actually admit mistakes and actually make changes to fight results. Which completely blows my ******* mind after seeing other commissions operate. Usually when you challenge any authority with any bit of power, the individual or organization will take that as a threat or challenge to their authority. We see this with police officers alot when they are dead wrong in a situation and multiple people are questioning their authority and they dig their heels in deeper because they can't possibly be wrong.It takes real leadership to admit mistakes and a clear mind to admit fault and change. I have been fortunate enough with my sports, military and current job to learn that and be humble enough to admit and change.Back from my rant, this is why I favor NJ so much and what their doing.

posts.author: CheckLeftHook
posts.body: @HiDefff >If any of this is true, does it change anyone's opinion of the situation? For me no it doesn't. He's a trained fighter, he's supposed to show discipline and try to deescalate instead of immediately go in for an attack as he gets in a guys face. Unless the guy threw the first punch or said something like "I'll ******* kill you" I just don't see what warranted Joe to make the first move, and it's nothing to do with some **** like "don't hurt people" or "he has a freedom of speech", nah it's more to me that he's stupid enough to risk jail time and losing his freedom just over some ego ********.Look I get that you shouldn't take people's **** and you should be able to have peace when you're out and about even at a place like a bar, but as a trained fighter he needs to have more discipline than just going immediately for a knockout next time. I'll **** on Joe as hard as I **** on Conor for hitting the old guy. That level of response from someone who trains to knock people out should be frowned upon. You're using your training going from 0 to 100 on someone who is of very little threat to you. Hindsight might be 20/20 but give me a break, how many instances are there where all of these highly trained people are legit in danger from facing off against these average Joe's? Honestly the way I see it is, if you want to scrap then go to the ******* gym. It's literally legal for you to beat the **** out of people there. To use some armchair psychology for a moment, I'd say that if you're just out and about as a proven professional fighter and you look to knock out random dudes who are mouthing off then that tells me you don't want any competition or resistance, you want an easy one and done scenario to make yourself feel good, feed that ego a bit. It's weak af. Get a new hobby and learn to relax.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: OK, so I've got this idea for a forum game. It will be a long term one so those of you who like short term games probably won't enjoy it but it's not meant to be that sort of game. Anyway, the idea is this: UFC: Avoid The Cut! Every week, the people who agreed to take part in the game, make their picks in the UFC event for that week and the one with the worst score that week (normal Tapology scoring system) is out of the game (users with joint worst scores will also be out). So for instance, if 30 people agreed to take part then by the 2nd week we'd be down to 29 players, 3rd week we'd be down to 28 and so on. All I'd need to do is just check the UFC scores of each user and put them in an excel sheet and all you'd need to do is just make UFC picks each week (which I'm assuming most users do anyway). I'd be doing this game in a new forum thread. If I were to do it then I'd be thinking of starting it on the UFC 265 event to give me time to round up players for it. posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: Just to clarify, the points don't build up over the course of the season as each user that remains will start again on 0 points for each UFC event so all you need to do is just not have the worst score for that week. This means that if you're in bad form every week but just surviving then you don't need to worry about anything other than the next card. Equally, this means that if you're in good form then one bad week could be the end of your run. I feel that it would make it more unpredictable as if the points built up over every week then by the time we were down to the final 2, one user might have a 1,000 point advantage which would take the competitiveness out of the final stages.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @XconchrisI'd never heard of her before this whole thing so I've no idea what sort of athlete she's normally like but the way I look at it is like this: She's the only person who knows what's going on inside her head so she's the only person in a position to decide what's right for her. I'm sure she is a highly motivated athlete as getting to the Olympics and winning gold medals isn't exactly something you can do in your spare time but if she decides that leaving all that to focus on her mental health is the right thing to do, then that's her call. I'm sure it sucks for her teammates in the team events though and I do feel sorry for them but that's the only valid criticism that I can think of in this situation.

posts.author: stuthehistoryguy
posts.body: On Biles: I cannot remember any precedent for this, so it's hard to say.However, to stretch logic a little bit, this reminds me a little of how elite starting pitchers were used decades ago, specifically Sandy Koufax. Dodger management was adamant that they couldn't allow Koufax to be a "seven-inning pitcher". They needed him to pitch effectively for nine innings, even with the arthritis and circulatory issues he was having. Koufax was brilliant, but he pitched his last game at age 30, saying that he may lose the use of his arm if he stayed in the game. Sports medicine has learned a lot about how to protect pitchers against career-ending injury and catastrophic chronic conditions, and today complete games are about as common as me having a threesome with two Brazilian models. I mean, they happen, but they're rare. In today's conditions, Koufax would have had a much better shot at a normal career - because sports medicine knows a lot more than it did in 1966.Perhaps in fifty years we will have learned more about athletes and mental health and recognize how many careers - and lives - were ruined by "toughing it out". Or maybe not. It's difficult to tell, and damned if I have enough background to say with my pissant Big 10 psych degree. I know what my gut tells me, but my gut is kind of an idiot sometimes. Two weeks ago, we lost a big account at work and a friend of mine was murdered, so I quite sensibly took Friday afternoon off. That's not the same as the Olympics, but Biles already has more hardware than True Value, and Lee won the Gold anyway. It will be interesting to see how we look at it five years from now. * Edited at 07.29.2021, 1:21 PM ET *

posts.author: stuthehistoryguy
posts.body: Yes. I am reminded of the time that General Patton slapped a soldier with "battle fatigue", calling him a coward. It later turned out that the soldier had been suffering from malaria and had been trying to keep it together physically for months. That's the thing with psychology - the mind and body aren't the separate entities that we often imagine them to be. I knew a guy who was utterly crashing, saw no reason to keep on living. Then he had a glass of orange juice and some snack crackers. In his case, that was all it took.And thank you for your kind words, Fisty. It was a shock. Jose was as nice a guy as you're likely to meet, and the circumstances were horrific. His son had schizophrenia, and Jose had gotten him work on a local construction crew rehabbing a dilapidated apartment complex. Then the son decompensated.Omaha man charged in fatal shooting of his father had stabbed stepfather years earlier | Crime | ontownmedia.com * Edited at 07.29.2021, 3:42 PM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @JoshjeffordsI don't have them too often but us Brits love a good crumpet and scones with butter! As for coverings, I usually just stick with butter but jam is fairly common and if you're fancy, you might put syrup on some things. CrumpetSconesGeneral biscuits of all sorts of varieties are very common, it just depends on how you like them. For me, you can't beat a good chocolate digestive, though I don't like dunking them in tea which has caused many passive aggressive arguments in the work break room.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @JoshjeffordsAlso, Jaffa cakes are huge over here as well. They are sponge on the bottom with a thin chocolate covering with jelly in the middle (this is about the only time I consume jelly in my diet). Just don't start THAT argument over whether they are a cake or a biscuit! That's just asking for trouble! Seriously, there was a whole legal case about it! McVities (who makes them officially but there are many knock off brands) argued that they were cakes so they could pay less VAT (Value Added Tax) as cakes are subject to less VAT than biscuits. The Jaffa Cakes Wikipedia page has a section on the legal case.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaffa_Cakes#Legal_status

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: I don't see any solution to that issue either. Let's say the UFC and Bellator release the fighter. Their will always be a large number of shady car salesman promotions willing to give that fighter is a danger to himself a chance. The Athletic commissions can only do so much. They could take away your licence to fight if your medically unfit. But they can't stop the free choice of a fighter who wishes to continue when he should retire. The only way really to stop the issue is to stop the money. Fans would have to collectively stop watching and paying for the retired fighters fights. If the promotion doesn't make money, they would be unable to host the individual. But there's no chance of that happening. People are not united enough and basically only care about themselves. So I really don't see anything that could stop a over the hill fighter who poses self harm from continuing to fighter long past his experation date.

posts.author: Gregory
posts.body: @Xcon in a perfect world, where the athletic commissions were all well-run and working with each other, they could all jointly agree on having a national list of fighters who are ineligible for further combat sports. And then the only place that fighter could go compete would be outside the country, or in unsanctioned sports (e.g. slap fighting or something else that the commission doesn't have jurisdiction over). I think this would go a long way to stopping unfit fighters from continuing to fight into dangerous territory. Even though the fighter could still work around it, their choices would be a lot more limited.But I think that still leaves 2 huge problems:1) I don't see it ever happening. I don't think all the commissions are well-run and looking to work with each other to this extent.2) How do you define when a fighter is unfit? Is Nick Diaz unfit? He was slurring and making very little sense in his long interview with Ariel Helwani in 2019. Is that because he's punch drunk and has early signs of substantial CTE? Or is it because he's Nick Diaz and he was high as a kite or just having some other problem that day. I'm not sure what set of criteria you'd apply to say when a fighter is unfit/ineligible.

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: @Greg. I don't have a answer to that. That is something athletic commissions and regulators need to come together and figure out. It will be a grey area and should be judged on a case to case basis. Probably needs opinions from medical experts as the primary decision maker on who is unfit.Also i agree that I have very little faith in any bureaucracy like a athletic commission. Except New Jersey. They are run very well. As I stated earlier, I don't see anyway to avoid this catastrophe. Unless people in 2021 decided to stop sending money to these abominations like the promotion Thriller. Get ready for Ken Shamrock vs Justin Bieber coming on PPV

posts.author: Gregory
posts.body: @harris Taking a quick look, I can see your requests/messages in our support system, but we get a LOT of messages so cannot always respond to them quickly. A betting site relying on Tapology to post Submission Underground results within 2 days ... as verification of the event results being legit... is insane. Talk about laziness from the bookmakers. If you are a bookie with cash hinging on the bets you made available, how about you assign someone to watch the event and verify the results?!?Tapology works to EVENTUALLY get all these results posted as accurately as possible. But there is an overwhelming amount of combat sports events that take place around the world. Tapology does not act as a bookie for any of them. We make no commitment to getting results from a grappling event posted so quickly. Sometimes we might, other times we might not. And the smaller the event, the more likely it is we might have made a mistake in the results we've posted. We rely on all kinds of sources and sometimes we post things that are incorrect until it all gets sorted out. edit, to clarify, this rant is mostly directed at any crazy/sketch bookmakers out there, not as a criticism of anyone else in this thread! * Edited at 08.16.2021, 12:55 PM ET *

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: Ill argue against legalizing one technique that @Mikey mentioned. Knees to a grounded opponent. This is illegal for a reason. When you get a knee in the standing position, the head has movement to move backwards. The reason those knees are illegal on the ground is because the head has no way of moving backwards and thus breaking your neck and killing or paralyzing yourself. So when a opponent shoots in for a take down and the other fighter stuffs the take down, we all deep down inside want him to throw that knee to that bent over fighter PrideFc style and make him advance his position or let go/go unconscious. If he got a knee in that position his head would compress into his spine and very bad **** would happen. Thus punching to the spine and back of the head is illegal. Injury is expected but we try not to cause paralysis or death * Edited at 09.05.2021, 12:21 PM ET *

posts.author: stuthehistoryguy
posts.body: In-ring techniques aside, I'd feel a lot better about the sport without the extreme weight cuts. I know that cutting weight has been a part of most of y'all's lives for your entire time in combat sports, but I'd rather have a contest that depends more on skill and less on whose metabolism more efficiently sheds water weight. Put another way, I'd love to live in a world where folks fought reasonably close to their ideal weight, not the lowest weight they can get to. I don't like to see athletes going into renal failure before a bout, and studies indicate that dehydration increases the risk of brain damage. * Edited at 09.07.2021, 3:45 PM ET *

posts.author: Tieoken
posts.body: Weight cutting is not diet control. Dieting and losing body fat is not weight cutting. Weight cutting is drinking 3 gallons of water on Monday, drinking no water on Thursday and then hopping in a sauna Friday morning until you’re 15+ pounds lighter than you were on Monday.Eating chicken and broccoli and skipping cake has nothing to do with “cutting”

posts.author: FistyMcKickerChoke
posts.body: But passing out builds character!

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Again I said MMA takes it to an extreme but of course your RUhhhhtarded ass struggled to read one sentence and ran with it. And come on Austin don't play dumb...........any wrestler from 6th grade on or any fighter who cuts weight......also diets or watches their weight? If not the cut is that much harder. If it's so detrimental then why are middle school kids allowed to do it?

posts.author: Tieoken
posts.body: Oh for sure, there is both elements in "making weight" for instance my schedule usually looks like this:Sunday: 3 gallons of water, normal salt, normal carbs (i.e healthy but no restrictions)Monday: 2.5 gallons of water, normal salt, normal carbsTeusday: 2 gallons of water, normal salt, 1/2 carbsWednesday: 1 gallon of water, normal salt, no carbsThursday: 1/2 gallon water (stop drinking and eating 24 hours before weigh-ins) no salt, no carbs.Friday: No water, no salt, no carbs, no food. Run/jog (1-3miles) in full suit. Sauna (30 minutes in 5 minutes out) until weight is made.Most I've ever lost is 27 pounds Sunday to Friday. While the dieting aspect is healthy and normal, the severe dehydration and lost of salts from the body is very unhealthy and causes severe damage to the brain and kidneys (as well as just about every organ of the body).I am in graduate school researching concussions and their associated damage to the brain. I can promise you that just because we don't see immediate chronic effects, individuals are paying the price across their lifetimes with severely increased risk of: stroke, alzheimers, dementia, PTSD, mania, anxiety, and chronic depression.Saltatory conduction is the ENTIRE basis by which our neurons communicate inside our brain. Imagine what you're doing by eliminating those salts and lowering your baseline like we do in weight cutting. Now imagine you lose water which provides the bulk of your ECF and blood-brain-barrier protection. NOW imagine you're a developing 14 year old kid with 4-5 times more neurocognitive energy requirements of an adults and you're doing these things...? Trust me weight cutting is ****** up, but people just don't know why. I'm not mad at any coaches or anyone because nobody has educated them. The field is so far behind in these areas and public outreach is just trash. Think about the amount of misinformation and lack of education about a ******* world-wide vaccine. There's even less knowledge and public understanding about concussions.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: OK. Here's my take on this. For those of you who remember my transgender fighters post in that UFC thread a few months back, my opinion on the issue is largely unchanged. Here's the link for anyone who wants a reminder as I'm not typing all that out again. I'd love for transgender women to compete with cis-women if they could do so on a level playing field in regards to their physicality. I don't think were at that stage yet and truthfully, I don't know enough about the science to try and guess when that might be possible but I really do hope that happens. As for this particular thread, there are a couple of points that stick out to me. Firstly @Xconchris said that if a cis woman knowingly agrees to fight a transgender woman then they should fight as that's an agreement between both people. I 100% agree with this and given that I don't believe that we're at the scientific level to equalise the differences via surgery or medicine, I think that finding cis women who are willing to fight trans women is about the only realistic way of including transgender people in competitive combat sports right now.Now for the mis-gendering issue. I personally will chose to call transgender women "women" and transgender men "men". My logic behind that is that calling someone by the pronoun they prefer doesn't harm me or anyone else in any way. As for @Xconchris's take on it, I disagree with him but I'm certainly not going to try and police his or anyone else's opinions just because they differ from my own. I guess the real issue I had with the pronoun debate in this thread was that it distracted from the fight and the following discussion about how or if transgender women should compete in combat sports as I feel that would've made for an amazing and much needed debate about the issue if we could refrain from personal attacks. But instead it descended into a "what do we call them?" argument filled with personal attacks rather than a discussion filled with great points from both sides. I guess my point is that it became an argument over pronouns rather than a debate about the relationship between the transgender community and combat sports. I felt that side argument really distracted from what could've been an amazing debate.

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: @Stephen. I agree with some of those points. But I won't ever agree that there will ever be a fair playing field. Waiting for science is a dream. The only thing science will confirm is just how unfair and unequal the difference between men and women are. And also how bad women are getting screwed over. We don't even need to wait for science. It proves it now that it's a unfair advantage. if you polled every female UFC fighter you will literally get almost zero who agree they should fight a transgender man. Because they have common sense. I would say transgender fighters should have their own league but much like female heavy weights, you won't find many. That's unfortunate but that's life.

Do you guys really think deeply when you call male a male? I don't but I have no problem with accepting anybody else's chosen pronouns. Anyways that's all I have to say. Hopefully it wasn't all over the place. * Edited at 09.12.2021, 9:03 PM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @XconchrisI agree that hoping science will one day equalise the playing field is more of a hope than an expectation and I'm certain that it won't happen in our lifetime but I refuse to rule out that it might one day be possible as you never know what technologies or in this case, medicines/surgeries are possible that we just don't know how to do yet. Science has and will continue to prove the many natural differences between men and women and it would certainly take something extremely artificial if it were to happen but again, anything's possible. @CheeseballThat's basically my view on it as well. It's a word and I've never understood why people (on both sides) make such a big fuss over it. EDIT: I also agree about the transgender league but then again, that would also split into different classes not just in weight but also in length of time as a transgender women. I wouldn't want to be the matchmaker that has to find an opponant for a transgender Featherweight who has been a transgender woman for 8 years and needs an opponant with the exact same criteria. * Edited at 09.12.2021, 9:02 PM ET *

posts.author: Casual008
posts.body: I know the situation is strange right now in the Flyweight division; Pantoja and Askar are both out for sometime with injuries and there's really no one else in the division who can be a title challenger but Figueiredo, but this trilogy bout can really stall or worsen the division depending on how it plays out. If Moreno wins, great! It closes this whole saga and essentially forces Figgy out of the title picture and we'll probably see him move up to Bantamweight like he's been hinting at. If Figgy wins however this makes it officially 1-1-1 and then what? We pretend the first two fights never happened and phase a young, charming fighter in Moreno out of the title picture till Figueiredo loses again, if he loses again, or does the UFC do it's first ever Championship tetralogy and stall the division up with this for another year while great prospects like Askar and Pantoja wait on the sidelines? I think this could kill a lot of the interest and hype in the Flyweight division that came out of the Moreno Championship if it truly falls back into Figueiredo's hands

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: @Xcon interesting. I don't have many Fighter-related stories, firsthand or otherwise. This is probably an opportune time to share one of the few I do have. This is a long story so I'll just leave the TL:DR here: A friend of mine is a well-known photographer who works in the US, was hired to photograph Cynthia Cavillo who turned out to smell so bad that he could smell her through a field of lavender, caused a quasi-supernatural insect event and the photographs he took of her also stunk.An acquaintance of mine is a credentialed photographer working out of the US, and is occasionally contracted to work on shoots for minor-and-up celebrities. We'll call him Larold, and he's an even bigger fan of MMA than myself. He was chuffed in Mid-2020 when he learned he'd been hired by Cynthia Cavillo to produce images for her instagram account, and did. I can't say exactly when and where this took place due to a need to preserve privacy, but I can say it happened in a field somewhere around Blanco, Texas, towards dusk.Initially everything was fairly Pedestrian. He'd Almost finished setting up his various apparatus when Cynthia's car pulled up by the roadside, about 40-50 feet away as he remembers it. She Stepped out and scanned around, her hand over her brow like a visor, and spotted him. He waved her on, and she had only just begun to walk towards when he first became aware of it:She was given to schtangk.To....to Schtangk!? Said I, not yet fully grasping the etymological intent of the term, delivered to me as it was in his rich creole--a bricolage of inflections loaned from places of the Deep South and Mid-west, but not being particular to any of them.Yessir. Powerful Schtangk.Larold is not prone to loquacity, and it did take some coaching and pressing on my behalf to extract a description of what this particular odour--for that is what 'Schtangk' referenced--was like in such a way as to satisfy my curiosity. His Testimony is as follows:"When we was boys, found an ol' homestead 'bandoned in the woods. Weren't much to look at, weren't much to find. Jus' a stack o' grime and ghosts. Bunch a broken down furniture and ol' liquor bottles, mos'ley. Friend o'mine, mason, he gets to ruttin' about in this ol' busted to bits pantry, fishes out a can o' crab. Still had the label. Dated 55'.Go on, I said.Well now mason gits to thinking he''l open her up, jus t'see. Takes out a pen knife, stabs down onnit. And as he did it a fierce gust o' juice spurts right on up all over his lip n 'eye. Worst schtangk I ever knowed--least, t'were. He went stone still for a moment then he blew his gritz right all over his boots. Ran outta there screamin' n' hollerin' like a snared daddy jackrabbit'.I see, I said.But that don't hardly do it. That don't hardly do it all. Now, down in the bayous there be a kind o' pool. Locals down that way yonder call it a 'Turd swamp'. Least the older men do, younguns call em' 'Dookie Ponds. Now these pools don't hold not cats or craws, see.They aint' holdin' no life t'all. Just the mud, sof'ly broiling schtangk and fairy fire like the devil hisself's own pot O' Gumbo. Schtangk'd peel the finish right offa Cadillac.He continued:Now let's say you rounded up the contents of a few pounds or so that 55' crab, and hauled an ol safe outta one o' them turd swamps.Then you took a couple o' used adult diapers--hard used that is, and made yoseself a big ol' sandwhich outta it all, and locked in that there safe. And then you went n' took that safe, and you sat it on a shore somewhere on the east coast, high-summer. And then you went n' lit a little fire 'neath it, let it run out. Well now, when you came back and opened the door o' that safe, n' maybe a day r' two, you'd be gittin' close to a schtangk bout that woman's.Engrossed as I was by his telling of advents, It only occurred to me in retrospect as he recanted this last passage that he had removed his wide-brimmed hat, placed it over his heart, and had spat energetically to one side. His voice, usually rather gruff, had become thinner and his eyes watery--whether as a consequence of some psychosomatic response at reliving the memory or otherwise, I cannot say.But that weren't all of it. Near as I could tell, and a'cause it seemed like a good reckoning, she'd drawn things outta the earth by the power o' that schtangk.What do you mean...things? I asked, by now thoroughly intrigued and having come to grips with his peculiar patois. Well now, she's moseyin on over--bearing down on me, that's t'say, and the closer she gets, the more that schtangk starts to feel like gravity. I can rightly feel mahself gittin forced to a knee. And I look about her, and she's closer now, p'haps 15 feet away.Close enough to see her 'stache.But you mean she was just getting very close--you mean that as an expression, like when one can 'see the whites of their eyes'? I interjected.Nossir. I mean I could. See. Her. Stache'.Jesus christ on the cross, I uttered. He went on:Did some work for ol' Don Fry back in 2003. Not a doubt in mah mind that woman's stache'd make that man's look like hairs on a peach. Bristliern' a hog's paw and coulda scoured the black offa a scalded skillet. Stache like that'd make ol' Dons **** shrink right up 'isside his belly. Held drops o sweat fatter'n jelly glass shakin on a cathouse chandelier.And then I lookdown t' the ground, and there's a river of ticks crawlin' across my boot, and the ground worms had come up too. I do misself a little honkey tonk, shake em' off. I look back up, and there she is, big as hell isself, trailin'the mightiest blow of flies I ever sawed. Yellows, cranes, hosses. And not flies only but the sqeeters and kissin' bugs, too. They's pourin' out the bushes and the mud cracks and every goddamn where,n' they knew where they's home was sho' as the rivers know there's be the sea. And that home were her.I could not help but imagine him stood there, lurched-over by mountains pooling the quiet dark of the encroaching Texas twilight, perhaps a star or two pricking through the dirty violet where a pale hunk of Gibbus moon hung half imploded like a hatched black widow's sac. Stoic, but wilting slowly as a green branch on a bed of coals. Had she worn a rotting gown of cabbage-moth wings pin-stitched in spider's silk, a rough crown of beetle shards and bat bones snagged in the oily hair falling over her eyes, this terrible Goddess of Miasmas? This creature whose hell-hym of stench had crooned from the rends and crevicature of the earth the blood-biting and boneless, the winged and toothed, and all the putrid, chitinous and vile life of the plain and prairie?'What did you do"!? I asked incredulously. By now a tiny heart had begun pounding under my collar.'Well now, I hadda think fast. It came to me that 'bout mile or two uproad, there be a lavender farm. Plenty of em' in Blanco. So I put my hand up as I would a stroppy steer, said it'd be best if wetook the show on the road down the way and catch the last light. I walked on 'head o' er and told her to drive behind me. She obliged. Bugs didn't let em up. They crawlin' all over that vehicle as though they was honeybees swarming an old queen's hive.Tweren't neerlah so bad takin em' shots in that lavender field, and it verily did mask the stangk somewhat, though it tweren't dandy niether. Schtankg was still strong as hell though I shot her from a good fitty feet away, for 'per-spec-tive', if you catch my meanin'. 'T'was like wipin yo' grandma's ass with a bag of potpourri. Now I'm old school. I still shoot the old way, with cell. No word o' lie, when I darkroomed those pictures and finished up, they bore the ghost of that schtangk them very selves.Although I wasn't present and cannot guarantee the veracity of this story, I can nonetheless confirm I have no reason to doubt its Authenticity, the teller of it being well known to me. I am sorry if this offends some people, I have simply done my best to report the facts as they came to me.

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: @Cheese there's definitely far too many belts. People complain about UFC having a virtual monopoly on MMA which is true but if fighters were able to freelance and weren't contractually obligated to fight only in one promotion, and could fight in any number of them, you'd get the same issue in MMA too. There'd be bazillions of belts and an ensuing cherry-picking festival for all time.The way the current market is structured means that in MMA, most of the time the best do fight the best in their primes. The price of that is that the labour relationship between the promotion and fighters is drastically unfair for fighters and massively favours the promotion. Removing contractual lock ins for fighters would mean they made more money (probably) and would severely limit the ability of UFC to leverage them, thus placing far more power in the hands of the fighters.However, handing a person power does not mean they will use it well, and this is never more true than in a commercial context. Fighters would gain a lot more power, and the fans would see far less of the fights they wanted to see when they wanted to see them. Or more accurately, they will not use it in a way that the consumer base wants them to use it, because people will always exercise agency in such a way as to secure the best outcome for themselves, and this only occasionally aligns with the desired outcomes of others. So I suppose the moral of the story is that some things are inevitable, and people need to be careful what they wish for. * Edited at 09.26.2021, 4:28 PM ET *

posts.author: Mikey1903
posts.body: For boxing I'd say the Ring Magazine ranking is pretty solid. The no. 1 in these ranks here https://www.ringtv.com/ratings/ is the best in the world (of course rankings are always subjective). My only issue with Ring is that they sold a huge stake to Golden Boy Promotions, and you can see how that can be trouble. I have noticed that sometimes a Golden Boy prometed boxer sneaks into the top 10! I've noticed this is sometimes an issue with ESPN boxing rankings, sometimes a Top Rank (broadcast on ESPN) guy sneaks into the top 10! But still, overall I'd say they're pretty solid.After having said that though I remember who I would recommend as the place for completely unbiased boxing ranks. The Transnational Boxing Rankings. No promoters involved, no journalists who work for Promotions, no belt organisations. Just honest ranks from over 50 members (all unaffiliated journalists and record keepers) https://tbrb.org/If you want to see who the actual top boxers in every weight class are, regardless of belts and promotions, this is where I'd recommend. Or you could always ask me!!!! But I may be a little bias and convince you that Chocolatito is the greatest Flyweight ever to live, P4P no. 1 and no. 1 current Flyweight, Super Flyweight and Bantamweight all at the same time !!!

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Yeah, no doubt Ring is far and away the best. But I'll never forgive them for this ridiculous ********!Roman is an absolute savage, never a dull moment! But I don't know if he fought Inoue now he'd beat him. Though I'd be rooting for him and he definitely could. That's ANOTHER fight that should have happened already and probably never will.P4P is weird and it gets hard to think about because we'll never know if guys like Jimmy Wilde would have beaten the flyweights of today. But it's safe to say that someone who KO'ed nearly 100 people or whatever would have had a shot. You know I used to own an antique boxing glove ( gifted it) that I won for a pittance on ebay with a snipe years ago. It was post 1950 because it was dyed. but whatever was in there wasn't foam. Not sure if it was horeshair or not, didn't feel like it but the **** would I know. Anyway, when you handled it you could tell Boxing used to be a different deal. The leather was really really thick. I guess they didn't have the tech back thin to make it as thin as they do now. And you could sure as **** move whatever was in there around VERY liberally, and it wouldn't have been hard to completely change the weighting or shimmy it totally off the knuckles in a way that just can't be done with injection-moulded padding today. And I bet they did, too. It would have been unpolicable. Like I say that was a post 50's glove and the ones older than that would have been even more basic. So I'm not saying these insane KO records like Ray Robinson's and Wilde's are down to the gloves purely...but I'm not going to ******** and say I don't think it had a whoooole lot to do with it either, and if I had to lay money on it, I'd bet that if you put the current gen of boxers on those glorified leather hand wraps a lot more people would be getting knocked out today too.

posts.author: Tieoken
posts.body: I highly suggest attempting to read the following review article, it is well done and designed to be informative without being biased. Performed by people whose work I respect. There is a lot of scientific literature about Ivermectin that has been formally withdrawn in the last year because it's been put under the spotlight and a lot of falsified data has arisen, but I'll include the most pertinent paragraph for a layperson below.https://doi.org/10.3389/fimmu.2021.663586"Notably, the clinical outcomes upon prescribing IVM on its own did not result in significantly improved outcomes for COVID-19 patients and nor should it be particularly encouraged (54). In fact, cross-resistance to other medications may be induced as a result of selective pressure resulting from a single medication (64). This may be a likely event as RNA viruses are well noted for their pronounced capacity for mutations, a finding which has already been established also for SARS-CoV-2 (65). Therefore, although IVM may contribute to the suppression of SARS-CoV-2 replication, it is important not to dismiss the risk of selecting for highly pathological and resistant viral strains when using a sole medication. That said, in a recent clinical trial that we have just concluded and is under review, we show that a single dose of IVM can significantly reduce the viral load in asymptomatic SARS-CoV-2 positive subjects. However, in these subjects, zinc and vitamin C were concomitantly used."

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: EDIT: I will not respond to this discussion any further, unless the person initiating is Teoken, who I can guarantee is capable of maintaining an adult discussion and works in a related industry.@Fisty I honestly don't understand that attitude. Wishing harm on people. Even if Ivermectin proves to be completely useless in treating covid, I don't understand why you'd kind of low key want people to die for having tried it. People resort to all kind of quack cures for all kinds of things all the time. Homeopathy is absolute bunk but is still more popular than it has any right to be, but I don't actively wish for people who subscribe to it to die because they tried to treat Diabetes by drinking milk that had an onion boiled in it or whatever.Regardless, it is not as though there isn't strong evidence that exists in favour of it as a treatment. And yes, while it is stupid to eat horse paste--ivermectin in paste form that is used to de-worm horses-- it is also as equally ridiculous and unhelpful to define Ivermectin as 'Horse Paste'. Ivermectin is an FDA approved drug used to treat humans commonly. And again, you can get a script for it and pick it up from a chemist if you like. It is already regarded as an effective treatment against Dengue and the Flu.I don't truly know whether it works or it doesn't. But if it does, I don't want it to be stonewalled because Pfizer can't turn a dime out of it or for otherwise political reasons. And don't think Pfizer isn't capable or doesn't have the wherewithal to do it either. They were handed down the largest corporate fine in History for falsifying information relating to the pain medication Bextra.https://abcnews.go.com/Business/pfizer-fined-23-billion-illegal-marketing-off-label/story?id=8477617I know this probably didn't occur to you as you mulled this over for 5 seconds at a monster truck rally, checked what Stephen colbert had to say about it then formulated a 'reasoned' opinion, but just because a drug is used to treat animals doesn't mean that it cannot be used to treat humans also. Virtually every drug you have ever taken was at one point tested on an animal. Including acid, which would have made for an interesting lab report....For example, veterinarians often prescribe an anti-depressant to dogs and cats that is also prescribed to humans. It is called prozac.Right now Pfizer is working on a covid treatment in tablet form that works very very suspiciously like the way Ivermectin is thought to as a the****utic: As a protease inhibitor/ blocker of viral replicase.Anyway, as I said originally, the jury is still out. There is no scientific consensus that it doesn't work, and you will not have to look hard to find a very convincing article in a peer-reviewed journal by impartial researchers suggesting that it does. For example:https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0166354220302011Abstract Although several clinical trials are now underway to test possible therapies, the worldwide response to the COVID-19 outbreak has been largely limited to monitoring/containment. We report here that Ivermectin, an FDA-approved anti-parasitic previously shown to have broad-spectrum anti-viral activity in vitro, is an inhibitor of the causative virus (SARS-CoV-2), with a single addition to Vero-hSLAM cells 2 h post infection with SARS-CoV-2 able to effect ~5000-fold reduction in viral RNA at 48 h. Ivermectin therefore warrants further investigation for possible benefits in humans.To test the antiviral activity of ivermectin towards SARS-CoV-2, we infected Vero/hSLAM cells with SARS-CoV-2 isolate Australia/VIC01/2020 at an MOI of 0.1 for 2 h, followed by the addition of 5 μM ivermectin. Supernatant and cell pellets were harvested at days 0–3 and analysed by RT-PCR for the replication of SARS-CoV-2 RNA (Fig. 1A/B). At 24 h, there was a 93% reduction in viral RNA present in the supernatant (indicative of released virions) of samples treated with ivermectin compared to the vehicle DMSO. Similarly a 99.8% reduction in cell-associated viral RNA (indicative of unreleased and unpackaged virions) was observed with ivermectin treatment. By 48 h this effect increased to an ~5000-fold reduction of viral RNA in ivermectin-treated compared to control samples, indicating that ivermectin treatment resulted in the effective loss of essentially all viral material by 48 h. Consistent with this idea, no further reduction in viral RNA was observed at 72 h. As we have observed previously (Lundberg et al., 2013; Tay et al., 2013; Wagstaff et al., 2012), no toxicity of ivermectin was observed at any of the timepoints tested, in either the sample wells or in parallel tested drug alone samples. * Edited at 10.01.2021, 2:32 PM ET *

posts.author: Tieoken
posts.body: So, for edification purposes of the larger general audience I wanted to highlight two major issues when reading scientific literature. Firstly the valuation of scientific opinion on any human disease goes as follows:Trash opinion article by moron with no backgroundBiased opinion article by field expert who is probably getting paid to be a shillGenuine opinion article by a field expert.Scientific article (in vitro)Scientific article (in vivo model organism)Scientific meta-review (in human)This distinction is important because so many times we hear, "researchers cure AIDs.." or "Scientists have cured breast cancer..." But really it's just a representation in a model organism. And there are a variety of reasons why I can kill cancer in a rat and not a human, and even more reasons why I can cure a cell culture of almost any disease but am unable to in humans.The second piece I'll offer up is that even in peer-reviewed scientific literature, there's layers to the publishers. Just like in news you have AP, Bloomburg, Fox news, Vice, and the New York Post. In science you have all flavors of publishers, the most common method of distinction is to look at the "Impact Factor" of a journal. That is a good measure of how many scientific researchers are using that publishers scientific contributions. A low impact factor (<5) means that they are either a brand new, or bad journal.____________________Now on to a rational response. Ivermectin is a broad sprectrum anti-viral. The main caution I would give is that human immune response is slightly more complex than horses, and we also lives signfiicantly longer. Many therapies that work in shorter lived animals (rats/mice/horses/etc) won't work in humans because they cause a level of stress that only shows itself in later life. Whether Ivermectin falls into this category nobody knows, but it's definitely a weird pitch to just inject yourself with it if you're not on your last leg. If someone is critical the doctor will ask the next of kin or caretaker if they are allowed to try "experimental treatment" as a last-ditch effort. That's where you could maybe chalk someone up with ivermectin or any of these new drugs that haven't been validated.I am 100% convinced that ivermectin can reduce viral load (of Covid and other viruses). But I'm not an expert enough in the field to understand it's biomolecular pathway to say that it's a harmless drug to humans. I'm also not convinced that it can reduce viral load enough to be a potent therapy for Covid.

Her achievement was recognised with the sportswoman of the year award at University of Otago and OUSA Blues and Golds Awards on Tuesday.In a short statement to the Otago Daily Times Hubbard said she was ''grateful for all of the support and kindness received from the teaching staff and students at Otago University''.''It is not possible for athletes to complete at the Olympic level without the encouragement and aroha of friends, family and supporters.https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/laurel-hubbard-wins-university-of-otago-sportswoman-of-the-year-award/MC7H4ZWWSL2L7BGUHMLDA4Y3ME/

posts.body: I'm of the opinion that USADA hasn't actually cleaned up the sport it's just gave it an air of legitimacy and nothing more. USADA's also used in Boxing, and they were involved in one of the biggest dopping controversies in the sport's history when for dubious reasons USADA allowed Mayweather to use IV drips just a day before the Pacquiao superfight. The reason IVs are banned is because it's a great way to "mask" trace amounts of PEDs still in your body. USADA and Team Mayweather stated that it was for the purpose of rehydration, but the IVs were about 16% of blood quantity replaced and at the weigh in he didn't look that thoroughly dehydrated. WADA does not allow that large of amount of fluids before competition because of how easy it is to flush and mask PED usage. On top of this NSAC was never told of this till after the fight, they have to also sanction and allow the use of IVs but were bypassed by USADA themselves. There's also a lot of sketchy details like the fact it was all done inside Mayweather's house and not administered at a hospital. The UFC is no cleaner then Boxing. * Edited at 10.04.2021, 9:43 PM ET *

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: I'd had only a vague recollection of these things, but was under the impression that the concept of mutual combat only applied to fistfights, not conflict involving weapons. Apparently that's not always the case. Or at least it isn't in Illinois.So there's been two recent examples where perpetrators who would normally have been charged with murder/violent assault have walked because the conflict was limited to two mutually engaging parties and did not affect bystanders or anyone otherwise not involved.Some gang members in Chicago got off as per this definition:Five men were taken into custody, with the police insider detailing that law enforcement were going to seek murder and aggravated battery charges against them.But by Sunday, they were all released – without charges.“Mutual combatants was cited as the reason for the rejection,” read the police report, according to the Sun-Times, adding that the suspects were not cooperating with investigators.Which is one thing, but something much worse happened: Two kids got into a dust up over something, and one of them literally brought a knife to fist fight. He ended up stabbing the other kid to death. And he will not face felony charges, as per the incident being defined as mutual combat.And that seems completely insane to me. Did this kid mutually consent to get into a fight with another kid wielding a knife? Because I strongly ******* doubt it.Am I missing something here or is this literally as completely bananas as I think it is? Does America have...what, PVP zones?Video about it

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Looking at Deontay as a fraud is looking at things the wrong way. There's nothing fraudulent about that right hand. There are punchers, and there are Boxers, and he's a puncher. Most of the time, a very good boxer will beat a very big puncher, and it's pretty much always the case that absolutely monstrous punchers cannot box well at all. If you look at Wilder's record, it looks pretty much exactly like Earnie Shavers' record--except not as extensive nor as good. KO after KO after KO after KO, until he starts getting the high ranked great boxers like Ali and Holmes, who he lost to. Shavers is regarded as the hardest puncher in Boxing History.Punchers are great, they're an invaluable part of Boxing and in a sense define what makes a great Boxer a great Boxer. They are the test.In fact despite how incredible yesterday's fight was, Fury actually made that fight a lot harder than it needed to be, and did not Box well or to his full potential. How many times Wilder was able to defend his title should tell you how much Boxing talent there is in his division and circuit at this point in time. Not a whole lot. * Edited at 10.10.2021, 4:28 PM ET *

posts.body: In all seriousness, @Fish/Baldric (and others) have told me that I should stop fighting cause I can't train and can't cut weight like the people who I fight do. And it's a legitimate point to make; why do I fight if I can't put all my energy into it?The answer is pretty simple, I'm an addict and I can't really stop. Training helps, but every time they offer me a fight I just kind of can't say no. So, I get the concern, and in a lot of ways you guys are right and how many people care is actually really touching. But I'm less concerned for my health for two reasons. First, I've never been KO'd and absorbed almost no damage, I lose most of my fights being held down because my wrestling is ass. Second, because I don't cut weight (anymore), my neurotrauma scale isn't any worse than someone playing catch-football once in a while.

posts.body: There's a difference between being genuinely disgusted or incensed about something somebody has done that is egregiously ethically bankrupt or illegal or caused real harm, and scouring the internet looking for trivialities to be outraged about to earn social credit points, to get attention, or to use as fuel for character assassination against people one doesn't like.
