People who bet the woke drum don't really care about the subjects nor the people they advocate for. They are just malignant narcissists exploiting victimhood under the guise of moral virtue for personal gain. There's nothing genuine underwriting what they do and no integrity. It doesn't come from an authentic place. They are garden-variety sociopaths, and in fact the relevant scientific literature points this out pretty clearly. Good article about here, abstract below:https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2020-46166-001 We investigate the consequences and predictors of emitting signals of victimhood and virtue. In our first three studies, we show that the virtuous victim signal can facilitate nonreciprocal resource transfer from others to the signaler. Next, we develop and validate a victim signaling scale that we combine with an established measure of virtue signaling to operationalize the virtuous victim construct. We show that individuals with Dark Triad traits—Machiavellianism, Narcissism, Psychopathy—more frequently signal virtuous victimhood, controlling for demographic and socioeconomic variables that are commonly associated with victimization in Western societies. In Study 5, we show that a specific dimension of Machiavellianism—amoral manipulation—and a form of narcissism that reflects a person’s belief in their superior prosociality predict more frequent virtuous victim signaling. Studies 3, 4, and 6 test our hypothesis that the frequency of emitting virtuous victim signal predicts a person’s willingness to engage in and endorse ethically questionable behaviors, such as lying to earn a bonus, intention to purchase counterfeit products and moral judgments of counterfeiters, and making exaggerated claims about being harmed in an organizational context. (PsycInfo Database Record (c) 2021 APA, all rights reserved)

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Carmen Mola, an author who until now has been presented as a female university professor writing under a pen name because of her desire for anonymity (something nobody would question since progressive critics would simply assume she's doing this to protect herself from online hate hate speech), revealed her true identity to the world when the main prize was awarded in the presence of the Spanish King.When the writer's name was called three men walked to the podium. It turns out, Mola was actually a collaboration between three Spanish TV writers (Mola is neither a woman nor an academic). Their names: Jorge Díaz, Agustín Martínez nor Antonio Mercero are academics. They have worked on scripts for shows including On Duty Pharmacy, Central Hospital and No Heaven Without Breasts."Carmen Mola is not, like all the lies we’ve been telling, a university professor," said Díaz on winning the prize. "We are three friends who one day four years ago decided to combine our talent to tell a story."The group of men won the prize for a book titled The Beast, a historical thriller set during the cholera epidemic in 1834. During an interview with a Spanish newspaper, one of them claimed that they "didn’t hide behind a woman, we hid behind a name.""I don’t know if a female pseudonym would sell more than a male one, I don’t have the faintest idea, but I doubt it," he added.Carmen Mola was said by the men’s publishers, Penguin Random House, to be the pseudonym of a female writer born in Madrid. Mola was also described as a forty-something mother of three children, who worked as a professor while writing crime thrillers in her spare time."Mola", or the men hiding behind Mola, has also been interviewed by the Spanish press, and a picture has been shared on her publisher’s website depicting a woman with her back to the camera who was identified as Mola.https://www.zerohedge.com/economics/female-writer-who-won-worlds-biggest-literary-prize-turns-out-be-three-men

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Livinha Souza vs. Randa Markos I was unusually shocked to find this terrible bout sullying an otherwise very high quality card. Just about fell off my chair. Imagine this:You take a girl out on a date. Her name is Lauren or Laura, or Kate, and she's a seven, but after a few flutes of verve clicque, a toot of powdered sugar and some fiddling with the dimmers she'll bump up to a hard eight, maybe an 8.5, in a pony-choke position over the duchess.You pull out all the stops. You cracked out the la nuit de l'homme, trimmed your hangnails, and napalmed out the last rebellion alliance of crabs and lice bunkered down in your netherhairs with a treatment that lightened your pubes by three shades. It's only meant to be used at the morgue. You bought it on the deep web with Shiba Inu coin.You even made a five dollar donation to the pink river dolphin conservation fund, and requested they call you back mindway through dinner with a promise of five dollars more. Both parties obliged. Truthfully your interests in such creatures extends only as far as your deep suspicion that they might not exist. Not outside of Disney's The Little Mermaid, or a Barbie Malibu beach party Box set that is. Unless David Attenborough has been trolling you since childhood, Dolphins are the color of smog in Camden and live in the sea. You're surely more likely to find one cadging a smoke or flashing the foot traffic in an overcoat on the subway than you are to find a pink freshwater variety. It has to be ********.Whether it's a scam or not is ancillary to the fact that you provided a credible alibi to the soul you do not possess, and as you smiled and nodded through commendations of your ecological sensitivity you were not remotely suspected of thinking that you hope they're real, because if they are, they probably taste like cherry starburst and there's a chance you can try some in a hand roll at the sushi vendor outside Tsukiji fish market. You'll ask the hardbody behind the counter about it next time you're in town. And maybe try and **** her again, too. On balance, it went swimmingly. The doorman was a midget--a delightfully unexpected surprise--and in his brass buttoned bellhop uniform and crop-cut he looked incredibly like a wind-up mechanical military tattoo doll, and on the way in you stopped and started fumbling around his back through his jacket:Sir, what are you--Sssssh. I'm tryin' to find the key.They Key?...Sir, I don't--Yeah. The key to spin ya up so ya start clanging' the cymbals!He shot you daggers that made you glad he wasn't part of the kitchen staff, because if so he'd have definitely lovingly moisturised his sack with your portion of Foix Gras, as you leaned over propped by an arm on your thigh, your other thrusting the middle finger not three inches from his nose, in tears, your wicker-basket frame shuddering with the effort of heaving out wheezy, pleuritic laughter. Covid? You could be tubercular. That Vietnamese Hooker definitely wasn't Kosher. Looked and smelled like she arrived in the false floor of a shipping crate packed with my little kitty calendars and dried tilapia. No way she was vet-checked.You smoothed it out. Shoved a bill with Hamilton's head on it into his top pocket, winked and told him a Lannister always pays his debts, and for a good 45 minutes held a conversation as someone other than yourself, which is the last person you'd have wanted to be. You know you're a ******. After dinner, you go to freshen up in the bathroom, take out the blacklight laser pointer that came with your model 3. It shows up, among other things, remnants of narcotics and liberated bodily fluids in wan blue light. You want to see how filthy the place is. Based on the price of the grub, your guess is very.As you shine it around, you're surprised to the place isn't lit up like lightning, until on the second sweep something catches your eye on the far corner of the wall. You mosey over to take a look. It's an arrow. And as you scroll further down the wall, there's another one, and another, and a single scrawled word: Follow.Interesting. UV reactive ink?The arrows run down the wall and across the floor, lead to the stall on the far side of the room. Inside they creep up behind the cistern and here, you find a dollar bill taped to the porcelain. From here, they continue on to a sick, yellowing aspidistra in a faux marble Grecian in the lobby. Another dollar bill, and another set of arrows leading to an out-of-service outhouse behind the building. The door has half fallen of its hinges, and in it's dejected state it seems to say:The main who build me did so with love and care in 1924. His name was Lewis and he had brilliantined hair and his wife baked rhubarb lattice pies and danced the Charleston,and he's gone now and I don't want anyone else to touch me. If you have to take a ****, go in the bushes, like the bums, and the stray wirehairs.The arrows continue hither and hitherto. They lead to nooks behind air conditioning units, to street light poles, to manholes, to the undersides of chairs--always leading to a dollar note, always promising another. In total, you've collected nine of them. Engrossed as you are with your nose buried in the breadcrumb trail, you walk right into a parked car, and tumble to the asphalt. You pick yourself up, dust your shoulders, looking left to right. The place is deserted, nobody saw. The vehicle you bumped into is the only car left in the lot. It's also a Tesla. Your Tesla. And the little blue arrows run right up the side of it, right through the window, which lies shattered and glittering on the leather of the passenger seat. There are arrows here, too, and they lead right on top of the centre console, and here, a last and final word is penned in thick uppercase lettering: Jackpot!Foreboding spreads like slow, chilly fire from your belly to your bones as you reach in slow motion to pop the lid, knowing you shouldn't, but as a man who bought GME at 390$ per share knowing you will Click. Nothing emerge or explodes. But there is something inside. Something that doesn't belong to you.It's a roll of banknotes. Thick, heavy. But as you begin to peel through it, you are made aware of the conceit. Only the first note, a single dollar, is authentic currency. The rest are simply paper receipts from the restaurant, wrapped over a cardboard toilet roll core and bound together with a rubber band. And you flick through the layers, vaguely thinking a total of ten dollars won't cover the broken window, you find a Polaroid. And in the dim light of the interior lighting, you can still clearly make out that it features the midget doorman, who is dressed as Genghis Khan and offering his erect penis, which is substantially larger than yours, to the woman whose dinner you bought not two hours prior and is made up like a horse, and in attitude suggesting she is about to imbibe it willingly. * Edited at 10.19.2021, 8:39 PM ET *

posts.author: Casual008
posts.body: Raulian Paiva is one of the toughest unranked fights you can take in that division. He's 3-2 in the UFC currently but if you look closely that loss to Kai-Kara France is highly contested and opinion was and is split down the middle on who actually won that fight. The doctor stoppage in the first in his loss to Bontorin was probably the correct call but that first round was competitive and if the referee hadn't kept breaking up the pace I wonder if Paiva would've even gotten cut like that. Paiva cuts Bontorin->Bontorin gets a take down while bleeding everywhere->Ref calls timeout->fight resumes on the feet and Paiva is getting the better of the exchange->Ref calls time out->Puts Paiva on bottom position like before the first time out but in a worst position->fight now again resumes->They scramble get up and something lands from a wild Bontorin on the break away that slices Paiva->Paiva is active and surviving well, scrambling on bottom->Ref calls his last time out. http://www.mmadecisions.com/decision/9738/Kai-Kara-France-vs-Raulian-Paiva * Edited at 10.21.2021, 3:05 PM ET *

posts.author: CrimsonChinGuru
posts.body: Something I gotta bring up is how much of a sham these mma gyms are don't get me wrong I love my gym, it's ran by the legendary Cosmo 'Goodboy' Alexandre who is a great guy but the fee of $2400 or $1800 if you pay straight up for the year then Cosmo makes you buy his shirts or you have to clean matts + just gets on you every class if you don't have one after practice then they are talking about raising the price of memberships which makes no sense since we literally have 1 instructor in there most of times unless bjj where other black belts help while rolling: the whole point of them raising the prices is supposedly due to the instructors who've already been here from the start + half of the time it's amateur fighters teaching the classes... Haven't even got to the worst parts; classes are like an hour where 30-40 mins is conditioning, other 30 mins is either bag work or practicing combos on a partners gloves then even worse is if you want to hit mitts it's an extra $100+ for an hour of hitting mitts with Cosmo. Can see why a lot of people don't become mma fighters or things of that nature, it's expensive and it comes off has you are just there to provide them money. I know obviously a man with 100+ fights should get paid for his skills but Holy God some people work 40+ hrs a week, have families to feed and bills to pay just want to train without spending a bag.Sorry about the rant but just feels people are more about making $$ then getting fighters to the next level.Would love to hear other opinions on the matter and if I'm just being a broke boy. * Edited at 10.22.2021, 7:04 PM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @Baldric EgglingI don't think Baldwin should face any charges. He's an actor, his job is to act. I wouldn't expect him to have to check his firearms before use (that's what armours are for) and there's a good chance that he wouldn't know how to check it safely so it would probably increase the chances of something like this happening if he attempted to. From what I've read, the armourer puts the loaded gun on a cart and one of the producers picks it up, not knowing it was loaded but also without checking it and shouts "cold gun" or something similar to indicate the gun is safe before handing it to Baldwin so maybe the producer that handed Baldwin the gun is at fault to an extent but I'm a bit confused as to why the armourer didn't check the gun was safe and then hand it to Baldwin herself. That seems like a much easier and safer procedure but I've never been in a movie or fired a gun so what do I know? * Edited at 10.24.2021, 6:42 PM ET *

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: He's not just an actor. He's a producer. And he was working in that capacity when this happened. By extension, that effectively means he's culpable for essentially everything that happens on set. It's not really a 'mens rea' issue. As I said originally, it's a negligence issue. It's not really any different from when a CEO signs off on something that causes a recall or an environmental disaster. CEO's can be criminally charged with corporate misconduct.There were allegedly safety concerns prior to the incident, and several members of the production crew walked off the set as a result. There were three accidental discharges in the days leading up to it. https://www.news10.com/top-stories/rust-camera-crew-left-set-in-protest-hours-before-alec-baldwin-fatal-shooting-report-says/ * Edited at 10.24.2021, 6:54 PM ET *

This is starting to sound like one of those tragedies caused by multiple people dropping the ball.

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: The offspring of hippos once owned by Colombian drug kingpin Pablo Escobar can be recognized as people or “interested persons” with legal rights in the U.S. following a federal court order.The case involves a lawsuit against the Colombian government over whether to kill or sterilize the hippos whose numbers are growing at a fast pace and pose a threat to biodiversity.An animal rights groups is hailing the order as a milestone victory in the long sought efforts to sway the U.S. justice system to grant animals personhood status. But the order won't carry any weight in Colombia where the hippos live, a legal expert said. “The ruling has no impact in Colombia because they only have an impact within their own territories. It will be the Colombian authorities who decide what to do with the hippos and not the American ones,” said Camilo Burbano Cifuentes, a criminal law professor at the Universidad Externado de Colombia.The “cocaine hippos” are descendants of animals that Escobar illegally imported to his Colombian ranch in the 1980s when he reigned over the country's drug trade. After his death in a 1993 shootout with authorities, the hippos were abandoned at the estate and left to thrive with no natural predators — their numbers have increased in the last eight years from 35 to somewhere between 65 and 80.https://www.yahoo.com/news/us-judge-pablo-escobars-cocaine-170913063.html * Edited at 10.25.2021, 3:55 PM ET *

posts.body: I'll throw an example myselfFrom my observations there are multiple types of confidence:Real, consciouss one, when fighter is sure of his capabilities, but acknowledge the dangers someone else is bringing in (Poirier, Jan for example)Fake, noisy - when fighter is trying to have an influence on an opponent before the fight, by screaming, being seen (Conor, Ion Cutelaba)Reckless - (Paddy, Kevin Lee)I think list goes on, but another thing I would like to point out, that I believe Conor has lost his confidence after first Poirier fight. That press conference before trilogy has shown that his skills in verbally shutting someone down have been lowered. He used to be so real and had flow with it, which I do not see now. posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: @Duck we're Australian. We're a lot more down-to-earth than our friends across the herring pond who tend to believe that America is God's last and finest creation and as Denizens of it they have a supreme place on the Globe. I can't say I blame them. America is a staggeringly beautiful and diverse country, not just geographically but culturally and Historically, more so than any other single nation on Earth. I'm not even sure I disagree with them.That said, next time you go to Melbourne, throw a rock. I'm sure you'll hit some blue-rinsed brainlet as bad or worse than anything the US has to offer. Sorry Melbournites, but Victoria sucks. And the Yarra isn't a river. It' a creek.Dave is right. Art should never be subjected to constraints--be they politcal or moral. At that point, Art ceases to be Art. What Militant progressives need to understand is that cancel culture is itself a form of tyranny. It seeks to organise society and culture via dictates, not the consent of the individual, or through any process of liberty or even consensus. The net result of cancel culture is not that liberty is increased. It is that it is erased.That is just the surface level of the problem, however. What it creates is a kind of...forced performance--a kind of state-sanctioned dramaturgy whereby everybody acts in ways that are inauthentic, because they are afraid to say and express anything out of lockstep with the oppressive 'ether' of what is permitted. The result is a complete ****genisation of culture and a stagnation of artistic and cultural development. But despite how apparent this is, everybody pretends that everything is normal despite knowing everything is wrong with every fibre of their being, and acts as if everything is fine. Eventually it gets to the point nobody can remember anything different. The Soviets had a term for this. They called it 'Hypernormalization'. It's a difficult concept to explain. As Solzhenistyn puts it: 'Not everything has name. Some things lead us into a real beyond words'.You know, I read something a while ago by a guy who was talking about how he had to read Anne Frank in Highschool, as part of learning about NAZIsm. The class were all reading it together quietly when suddenly the teacher shouted at one of the kids, and demanded he get up and come to the front of the room. When he got there, he berated him very angrily, and said he'd been talking to someone a seat or two down. The kid began to deny it feebly, but the teacher shouted him down, and demanded that if anyone disagreed, or wanted to defend him they should come forward--but they'd be in as much trouble as him. Everybody in the class knew this kid hadn't done anything, but they all sat there in silence. Eventually the teacher told the kid to get down to the principle's office.When he left the room, the teacher closed the door behind him, turned to the class and said: 'Did you see how easy that was'?The point is, there is something greater contained in tyranny than the individual act of it itself on a case by case basis. Especially when it is executed by Authority or under the guise of it. The effect extrapolates out like ripples from a stone thrown in a pool, and creates a climate of fear and paranoia that is itself a form of control. The freedom to do and make and say and think freely is something that exists in the West, but it is fragile, and it is never more than a generation away from extinction. It is an extremely rare exception to the overall trend of History. I can only hope that young people realise it, and will fight for its continuation. Sic semper Tyrannis. * Edited at 11.03.2021, 4:50 AM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: OK, in a nutshell it's basically a "Welcome to Tapology/Tapology help/assistance thread" to try and help new users settle in. A sort of interactive Q&A page. Basically, I got this idea because I've been followed by lots of new users over the last year and I always follow back and leave them a welcome message on their page but very few of them seem remain active members. I don't know the exact figures of how many new accounts are made per day but I'd guess it'd be surprisingly high. The thing is you don't see 99% of them. If they are choosing to remain virtually unknown then fair enough if that's how they want to enjoy the site but I think a lot of them might want to be more active but might be a bit shy or don't know how to break the ice, especially on the forums. I know when I originally created an account, my original plan was to use it soley for UFC and Bellator picks and I never intended to become as active as I have and it's only when @Hippie left a comment on my wall "Holy PINK!" (My AV was just a plain pink square at the time) that I realised that it could actually be a great place to have a laugh and talk about MMA.... and then it basically just snowballed from there. My point is not all new users are lucky enough to have someone reach out to them randomly with a joke or a welcome message or they may want to talk on the forums but don't really know how to approach it or may fear how there'll be perceived (I've seen many sites where new users get treated like complete **** just for having the audacity to disagree with a more established member). So this thread would basically be a safe space for new users where they can ask questions about how certain parts of the site work without fearing as coming across as stupid and to get to know some of the regulars in the process. As for the posts in it, I was thinking of maybe making posts that are quick guides on how to do things such as rankings, fantasy games, and stuff that the we use all the time but may be completely alien to a new user. Any feedback on what the right approach to a thread like this would be?

posts.body: Sounds like a great idea to me.

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: But seriously, anyone who needs a quick refresher on why socialism is always an unmitigated disaster which has led to the greatest human atrocities ever experienced needs look no further than Venezuela for a modern example. It was the premier economy of South America VERY recently before the socialist regime seized power. Richest nation in the region. Chavez bewitched the public with promises of such things as free healthcare and tertiary education, and was elected in a landslide.Of course, he never delivered on those promises because utopia doesn't exist, and proceeded to re-write the constitution and stack the high court. He picked every one of the newly added twelve justices.Sound somewhat similar to anything you might have heard being advocated for by one particular US party? Interestingly, it was actually rising inflation ( caused by plummeting oil prices) that made people sympathetic to his message in the beginning.... Then he nationalised everything. Banks, mines, farms. Everything. Of course, this is economic suicide, and the country went broke. Venezuela become a poverty-stricken, chaotic cesspit, and remains one under Chavez's protégé Maduro, who is a standard Dictator who rigs election and will probably never be removed from power without the use of force. The Bolivar is worth nothing. Street vendors make bags out of it for sale to tourists brave enough to risk getting kidnapped. and ransomed during their stay.This stuff aint' ancient History, people. And it goes the same way every single time. * Edited at 11.15.2021, 7:38 PM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @adrndc11Nope. That could make things much worse. Sure the world has a lot of problems but in the grand scheme of things, it could be way worse. Let's suppose you travelled back in time and forged the papers necessary for a young Adolf Hitler to get into art school so that he didn't go into politics. The result may be that Hitler just became an obscure artist while someone even more deranged but competent as a leader took charge which resulted in the Nazi's or a similar organisation actually succeeding in winning WW2. It could work the other way as well and everything turns out to be perfectly fine but why take the risk? And that's not taking into account the few good things to come out of WW2 such as the many technological advancements made during that time, originally to help the war effort. Que sera sera.

posts.author: phillip53
posts.body: you mean like nuclear weapons?

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @phillip53I was thinking more low scale such as improvements to aircraft safety and more reliable motor vehicles in terms of the positive things. Overall WW2 did more harm than good and many bad things came out of it but I'm just saying that there could've been an alternative reality that may have been way worse. Out of all the trillions of possible paths history could've taken that led us to where we are now, I think it's highly unlikely that we lived through the worst possible one imaginable.

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: @ Stephen you're not completely wrong. NAZIS invented night vision, the helicopter, and the rocket science that NASA would go on to develop their space programs with. Wernher von Braun is regarded as the father of rocketry and was given a pass by the US so long as he agreed to work with them. He was, along with Herman Kahn, one of the inspirations for Dr Strangelove in the Kubrick film Dr Strangelove: Or how I learned to stop worrying and love the atomic bomb.However, they also invented Meth and Sarin and perpetrated one of the greatest mass-scale atrocities ever committed. On balance, the technologies that came out of WW2 especially from Germany were bad for humanity, not good for it. It's definitely not a net positive thing or anything close to it. And really none of it had to do with some third reich enlightenment. The Germans have just always been very good at Science and Engineering. Long before WWII and even WWI Berlin was the cultural and scientific capital of Europe. It's almost certainly the case that the NAZIS hindered progress of all kinds rather than bolstered it. Germany still is the world leader in Engineering and Finance. It's actually pretty staggering that Germany is the premier economy of Europe considering it was basically a pile of rubble in 1945.The NAZIS also invented FANTA (in 1940). The German division of coca cola came up with it because Germany was embargoed by the US and subsequently couldn't import coke, and needed something to sell. This is probably the best evidence of corporate amorality there is. * Edited at 11.16.2021, 8:25 PM ET *

posts.author: stuthehistoryguy
posts.body: Catching up on the previous page: it's an ethical paradox that some of the greatest advancements of humanity come from absolute tragedy. There's a line of thought that the Renaissance was a direct consequence of the Black Death, for example, and, as Stephen alluded, it's difficult to read any books on modern advancements in science and the humanities without seeing the adjective "postwar" all over the place. In a way, the Norse story of Ragnarok is a parable of this sort of thing - response to the worst we can do sometimes produces the best we can do.That doesn't mean you root for World War III or anything. * Edited at 11.17.2021, 3:01 PM ET *

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Tapology has the same fundamental issues that Boxrec does: It lacks a way to filter variables and measure them for comparison against any given fighter's upcoming opponent on a single page. This wouldn't really bother me however every fighter's entry on this site has 318 graphs detailing information that isn't really useful to me ta all in making a pic (it's just chart junk), and virtually none that would be. There are no stats for TDS/SLPM/SAPM/TDD etc et al. I understand there's just not going to be that data for a lot of fighters out there, but there's a lot of fighters out there for whom stats do exist.I don't need a horizontal bar graph detailing wins/draws/losses over a career span.Nobody does. It's just straight up ****** Heuristics. If I'm having soup for dinner and there's no spoon, I can't make do with a fork.This is why I still use UFC fighter stat pages when comparing attributes between two fighters and not Tapology, It is literally exponentially easier and clearer for me to load two pages on the UFC site to compare stats between fighters than to do that here.Also I don't understand what the point of having backlinks to Sherdog and wikipedia and every other MMA related data site is. This achieves absolutely nothing and is utterly redundant. Why do I need to go to Sherdog or a fighter's wiki entry if that same information is contained on Tapology anyway? I get it generates site traffic but none of it adds anything to the user experience and probably just confuses new members. And while we're on it, why not backlink to UFC stats anyway? Propriety issues, I suppose. * Edited at 11.18.2021, 10:54 PM ET *

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: I always play a game with the few people who figure out the hobby i have of coins. I always ask them what makes a coin valuable. Almost none get the correct answer. Every once in a while someone will surprise me.The first answer, the age of the coin. The older the coin the more valuable. Thats a negative. People have buckets full of roman coins from 2k years ago. Age doesnt always create value. There are modern coins worth much more than older coins.Then the precious metal content. Also negative. While Gold, Silver and Platinum are base line indicators of value. The minimum price for that ounce of Gold coin is always the value of coin but doesnt have to create a higher premium.The Rarity of the coin. Also Negative. Watching Pawn stars one time, i remember the older guy saying i have a one of a kind dog turd in my back yard. That doesnt make it valuable making it less rare. Often times having a low mintage is bad for the coin because there is not a lot of people seeking to purchase it, driving the cost down. The more people want it the more valuable it becomesThe correct answer is actually Desire and a healthy marketplace. For example. If your planet is too close to the sun you burn and too far away you freeze. So the mintage numbers(rarity) of the coin must be a in goldilocks zone. That number is prevalent for the 1909 S VDB Lincoln Penny. It has roughly 484k minted. Theres a healthy marketplace. Now poor collectors could have a cheaper worn down version for low cost and high end collectors have fight over premium examples. Everyone can own one in different grades and its not flooded with coins or extremely rare.The other answer is Desire. And i use the diamond example for that. A sapphire is more rare then a Diamond. A Diamond is more expensive then a Sapphire. Why? Because "Diamond are Forever" The desire the own them and other shady people controlling the supply makes them more valuable. Now back to the 1909 S VDB penny. Many other coins have roughly the same mintage at 484k. But these are the 1st year of the Lincoln Penny and highly desirable. Mostly due to folklore and the significance of the coin.Supply and Demand baby!

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @RignoI used to be obsessed with the Match Attax cards when I was a kid but I don't collect them any more. I put a whole bunch of them up for sale on eBay once (about 500 or so average cards) starting at a tenner just to get rid of them. Not a single bid. I then tried selling them at various local car boot sales and I was getting about 50p per card (even for the not rare and damaged ones) and that's an average btw, I didn't set a price, I just let guys make offers and as long as they weren't really taking the piss, I let them go cheap. I got rid of most of them and ended up making about £370 over that summer. One other guy who was a serious collector told me I could've made even more at specialist fairs and websites but I didn't care about the money too much as I'd already made more than my original tenner lol! I'm still not 100% why local car boot sales ended up being a more successful marketplace than the biggest auction site on the internet though.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: I've still got loads of low value junk lying around, I really need to start getting back into doing local car boot sales. I used to do about one a week over the summer months for a few years straight but I haven't done one for about 2 years now.

posts.author: Fremen
posts.body: From what they have told on interviews and comments, this streaming service is running in like 7 or 8 european countries, they told us(the fans) that KSW.TV will still work for people not from these particular regions. But like Poland, Sweden etc. it is Viaplay only.Viaplay is cheaper and it includes not only KSW but like some football (US translator: soccer) leagues, TV series and movies. Viaplay plans expansion and is heading to new markets, possibly bigger ones.That is why KSW decided to do 12 events a year, to make sure that if you buy a monthly sub of this partnered platform - you will have what you buy it for, certainly.So they will most likely still allow you to watch it on ksw.tv if you are not from these couple of countries. Which also just made me realize I made a mistake saying that it changed its streaming site entirely, Ima edit it right now

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Of course you think you 'Know' you're smart, Fisty. I've never met a stupid person who didn't. It's classic Dunning-Kruger effect. Intelligent people understate their intelligence, and have lots of doubt. Stupid people overstate their intelligence and have lots of certainty. The Dunning–Kruger effect is a cognitive bias[1][2] stating that people with low ability at a task overestimate their own ability, and that people with high ability at a task underestimate their own ability.As described by social psychologists David Dunning and Justin Kruger, the bias results from an internal illusion in people of low ability and from an external misperception in people of high ability; that is, "the miscalibration of the incompetent stems from an error about the self, whereas the miscalibration of the highly competent stems from an error about others".[3] It is related to the cognitive bias of illusory superiority and comes from people's inability to recognize their lack of ability. Without the self-awareness of metacognition, people cannot objectively evaluate their level of competence. * Edited at 12.09.2021, 6:45 PM ET *

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: Concepts are more than definitions, Fisty--and you didn't even get that right, because nowhere is it suggested that the Dunning-Kruger effect applies to either a specific or broad field of 'Knowledge'. You are literally meta stupid. To wit:"If you're incompetent, you can't know you're incompetent ... The skills you need to produce a right answer are exactly the skills you need to recognize what a right answer is."[15]General Knowledge isn't the issue, Fisty. General Intelligence is the issue. It is impossible for the Dunning-Kruger effect to meet its definition, as a logical course, without making reference to some form of underlying standard, 'progenitor', or cause. That can only be intelligence or the lack thereof. This is because the absence of metacognition, which necessarily must be considered a form of intelligence, or is otherwise an indicator of it, is a requisite for meeting the definition of the Dunning-Kruger effect. If one were...rich, in metacognition, they would not overestimate their competence, and could not therefore meet the definition of the term. Or more simply, streams cannot rise above their sources. If Einstein had had never been introduced to Mathematics, he still would not have been stupid. Surely at least you can follow that? * Edited at 12.09.2021, 7:46 PM ET *

posts.author: Fremen
posts.body: Okay, so I'll continue my story from UFC threadLink to first part here: https://www.tapology.com/forum/threads/62087?page=50#post-996235How I met American in my small town?Well, where do I start.. I will share wider context first. To your scales - around 15-20 miles from me there is a American Military base (I won't tell what is it called, I value my privacy a little), they have been there for some time actually, I can often see them transporting things to their base and stuff, they standout driving these wide vehicles. One or two weeks ago there were like 20 to 30 trucks driving thru my street with containers(?) that had wrote "Potable water" and "U.S. Army property" on them. There is a little more I have about them being in my region but I can share that later.So, heading back to my story. How I met American and had a short dialog with them?My classmate had his dad in Polish military, he was probably not so low ranked, so my English teacher came to a very fun idea to make contact with them and invite them to our school (by them I mean American soldiers). They came to our school actually, from what I remember there were four of them. If I am not mistaken someone said they were (or one of them was) from air forces. In every year level of our middle school there was one language class that had more english lessons and they were divided into 2 separate groups (about 13 people each) so teacher can have more time with each student. If my memory doesn't fail me, they had like an hour meeting with each class (there were 3 years levels so 3 classes) And we had given them a table they sat next to, gave them some food. We were given a chance to speak to them, ask them some questions, make some interraction, which for me, as like 14 year old boy then - was pretty amazing - you hear american accent live, not on a movie lol, so surreal. Most of us were shy to even speak to them, but me and couple of classmates stood out and asked something. I was worried my accent would be too bad they would not understand me. I did not remember the questions but I do remember I asked one of them to armwrestle with me infront of everyone. Ofcourse he let me have some advantage, played with me.Well, for me as a guy who really likes english, a guy who watched hundreds of American movies, military movies as well - it was fascinating to see and hear something what I experienced through a screen before only. I mean, place where I live does not give me many opportunities to speak other languages. There are no foreigners that speak it. I'm not from bigger city like Warsaw or Krakow. I am very thankful for my english teacher who made it happen. Best teacher I've ever had, she put her heart into it, teacher who loves her work and seeing kids improve. Now I am 19, having senior year of high school, and I can honestly tell, she was best teacher Ive had. I mean all different subjects, not only English * Edited at 12.11.2021, 9:40 PM ET *

posts.body: KSW 65 Khalidov vs. Soldić2 main fights are Khalidov - Soldic and Parnasse - TorresKhalidov - SoldicPole with Chechen roots is absolute top legend and veteran in KSW. His style is unconventional. At 40 he delivered one of best knockouts MMA world has ever seen ( Soldic on the other hand is trying to become double champ by beating this legend. Soldic's best win is a rematch against Du Plessis 2-0 in the UFC), Soldic has an explosive style with a ticking bomb in his left hand. My personal pick would be Soldic even tho I share a massive sentiment to Mamed Khalidov. I do not imagine Polish MMA evolving without him. He was known by people not even interested in the sport. Next to Pudzianowski he has one of biggest influences in KSW progress as a company.Parnasse - TorresParnasse one of the biggest candidates of becoming UFC fighter. Lost to Torres in a first fight to in my opinion a weird knockout, man got knocked down by forearm or biceps, I don't remember the exact finish but it was weird af. changes I mentioned couple of weeks ago are already being seen. They started to sign new fighters already. Personally I think we will experience even more names that will come to the UFC from KSW. Michał Oleksiejczuk (4-2-1 UFC) and his coach have parted ways. Reason? Michał took head coach role during his brother's fight, so his coach decided he will no longer train them. You can remember him from his characteristic behaviour before and during the fight. I mean man was beating his fighters faces before to wake em up. During the fight one of loudest corners in MMA history.So there is now a huge possibility of Michał , his brother and more joining Jan Błachowicz's WCA gym as they both train in Warsaw. Both lightheavyweights. Haven't sparred or trained before couz their gyms were enemies. https://www.tapology.com/fightcenter/fighters/61212-michal-oleksiejczukhttps://www.tapology.com/fightcenter/fighters/71780-cezary-oleksiejczuk ( his brother) one month away from 22 years, FEN welterweight champion, 5 fight win streak.next week I'll probably drop some info about KSW 66 and second part of Polish prospects. She’s terrifying!” “There’s something staggering about that woman. I really do feel like she’s just some incredible, historical figure.” “She’s not a once in a lifetime, she’s once EVER.” “Unbelievable. Just unbelievable.” “To be able to call her fights is an honor. I don’t use that word lightly at all. It is a real honor.” “It’s an honor to call your fights. It really is. You’re the best ever, you’re just *stutters like the first time you asked a girl on a date* a.. a… a once in a lifetime human being.” “It’s like watching some unbelievable historical figure that’s never existed before.” “Man, I’m blown away tonight.” “I just want to tell you I’m just beyond honored to call this fight.” “Ronda Rousey is on another level. posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: Just out of curiosity for fans of superhero movies: what is the appeal behind them? I've seen several different ones now but I just can't get into the genre at all. Which is strange as I'll happily watch any kind of movie as long as the story and acting is good (except rom coms. Every rom com I've ever seen has been ****). Is it the over the top nature of them that provide escapism and contrasts to ordinary life? If that's the case then there are plenty of other movies in other genres that I can watch and escape from reality in. I dunno, I just get bored in them and struggle to build any kind of emotional connection with the characters whatsoever. Like when -SPOILER ALERT FOR THAT REALLY ******* LONG MOVIE I CAN'T REMBER THE NAME OF WITH THANOS IN IT- Iron Man died in that movie, I was in the cinema with my mates who were bawling their eyes out and I was just waiting for it to end so we could go get some food. And that was with having seen at least 2 of the previous Iron Man movies (definitely seen Iron Man 1 & 2 and I think I saw him in something else as well but don't know if it was an Iron man one or not) so I'd had plenty of time to get connected to him but didn't. At this point, I just think that I'm just never gonna "get" superhero movies.

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: Personally. I think they are just well made movies from Marvel. I Remember most of the super hero movies of the 90s and early 2000s being terrible. Also liked the Christopher Nolan movies for DC. I am not into comics or anything along those lines but Marvel makes awesome stuff.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @XconchrisIn terms of cinematic quality, the superhero movies I've seen have been mind blowing and the special effects are second to none. That stuff isn't important to me personally though as I've never seen one with a plot I could get into or characters I could get interested in. Even some of the acting has been about average IMO. For me, the acting and the story is what makes a good movie, I don't care if an obviously polystyrene prop tree randomly falls down in the background, the effects are trivial details as far as I'm concerned.

posts.author: Josh Jeffords
posts.body: StephenTerry I feel same way about superhero/dc movies, I don't have a special attachment to the UNI.To me they just represent an offshoot of over done fx action movies which can be fun but mostly target youth.For me the biggest problem is that they lose all balance and plausibility to even tell a story.I'm a huge scifi nerd and I like to be given stuff to think about not just get smacked in the eyes with stupid rays and explosions.My greatest enjoyment of them is watching comic nerds rip them apart thinking anyone cares at this point.Spawn is obviously the pinnacle of comic to movie ever GOAT!

Also I think they’re just good movies (not cinematic masterpieces or anything but always fun to watch).

posts.author: Cheeseball
posts.body: Well I would say you would have to grow up with the MCU and its depiction of Iron man to really understand the impact of his death. The problem for a newer fan is that Toney Stark's character arc is split between multiple movies (including some non Iron man movies like Avengers, Captain America Civil War, and Spider Homecoming). So you can't really just watch Iron man 1 & 2 then skip to Infinity War and expect to know *** is going on. If you do know the set up Infinity War feels more like a pay off rather then a mindless action movie.

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: @Baldric EgglingThat was the most accurate description of Croydon life I've ever read lol!!!!!So basically what happened last night was I got home late, flicked through the TV (and yes ITV and it's smaller versions were some of the channels I flicked through so you can tick that off your bingo list), decided there was nothing decent on and went into the kitchen and saw an unopened bottle of Jameson's Whisky I'd forgotten about and thought "**** it, I'll have a little bit" and a little bit became a little bit too many and came on here to kill the time until I fell asleep on the sofa. I was then woken up at about 10 by a loud thud caused by Hermes delivery driver tossing my mum's Christmas present against the front door. I went to collect it and waited until he was back in his van before flipping him the bird as I needed to vent my frustration somewhat directly at him while still not wanting to offend him as I'm nice like that. The present was undamaged luckily but if he was able to damage a scarf that was in a plastic bag, surrounded by half a kilo of polystyrene, inside a fairly sturdy cardboard box that I needed a boxcutter to open, using only the force of his throw then I'd have been genuinely impressed.

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: @Stephen Hermes? That's probably going to set a record for the most expensive scarf ever gifted in Croydon TBH lol

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: https://twitter.com/jakepaul/status/1477365412744552454?s=21 Really bothers me how much people think he’s doing a nice thing for the mma community. While it would be nice if Dana did this, I can’t believe people genuinely think jake paul cares about mma fighters. This is literally a a façade; if Dana agrees (which he never would) then he gets the Jorge fight he’s been begging for and if Dana says no, he gets to continue calling him a ************* on social media. It just annoys me that people think he’s being genuine here; it’s a business move just like everything he does. Not that I don’t think the fighters deserve better but I’m just really annoyed he’s using a serious situation as ammunition for himself.

posts.author: Xconchris
posts.body: @Ayymnaonnaise. I dont think of that the same way. While Jake Paul may big a huge *******, this is definitely not a smart business move. Agreeing to retire forever would be the worst business move, especially when he could pick and choose past thier prime mma fighters and other basketball players and rappers to easily KO.You could argue its a bluff because no way Dana White would ever except this because hes a corporate clone.How ever this started, Jake Paul and Dana White are at each others necks and looking for blood. Jake Paul finds a huge issue with his enemy and he is running with it. I Guarantee he really believes everything he typed about fighters about not being paid properly and deserving a higher portion of company profit. Basically anybody can see fighters are drastically underpaid.He may not be the spokesman you want, but often thats the one you need to push the pay or collective bargaining issue for fighters unionization. In regards to fighter pay and working together, he means exactly what he said, it was just grown for his dislike of fellow ****** Dana White

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: That’s a good point and he definitely believes it, because they are massively underpaid and treated like ****. But, believing and actually caring are two different things. I personally think he’s calling out the issue of fighter pay because it pisses off dana and also gets people talking about him and the Jorge fight. Also because he’s talking about the topic, it’s gonna get other people talking about the issue along with his message that he put out. I don’t think it’s an attack to change pay but to get more eyes on him. If he truly cared about fighter pay then he should just start his own mma promotion; he’s got the name and would surely get investors and also has the star power to sign fighters

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise

posts.author: Anonymous
posts.body: @Casual I don't care anymore TBH. Actually, I care enough to explain why it is I don't care.Imagine somebody came up to you and said they knew how to cure world hunger.Thier solution? People just started eating their own poo. And then they'll never be hungry again.UFC paying their roster 50% of earnings is equivalent to that. It doesn't warrant a response. Suffice to say if you don't understand why that is, you shouldn't even have an opinion on the subject.It's simply not possible under the current business model, and the kind of business model it would be possible under would result in a product you wouldn't pay for. Either that or it would result in a zero-sum game--in the case the redistributive model that is what is no doubt in your mind was enacted or otherwise. Perhaps UFC should consider the alternative; fractionalise payments based on consumer participation relative to individual fighters abilities to draw. I'm sure people like Chookagian would absolutely love that.If you want to see a watered down-version of what that would look like, by all means--sit through 6 hours of PFL to watch bums get paid one million dollars to beat other bums between 500 commercial interludes.People in this thread writing about this **** don't even know what they don't know. Home-brewed wisdom. Take your bongo drums and **** off back to the commune. * Edited at 01.02.2022, 2:43 AM ET *

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.body: Legit fight streams thread/PPV links thread.Basically, it's obvious that loads of us watch cards from our favourite local promotions and sometimes the streams get posted in the thread for that specific event but most of us don't subscribe to all the random smaller threads so they get missed. For instance, I don't subscribe to every minor American regional thread but if I happened to see the a free stream or cheap PPV for one of them and had nothing else to do then I'd probably be interested. So the idea is basically just have one thread where people who regularly watch those events could post the (legit only, none of those sketchy ones) stream links and say "hey btw this card is going down tonight and here's the link if you wanna watch" and anyone subscribed can just check out some fights that they might've missed otherwise by not subscribing to a million different threads.Obviously the major problem would be people (sometimes accidentally) posting dodgy links so obviously it'd be a bit of a pain to moderate from @Gregory's point of view and don't want to cause anymore stress for him lol which is why I'm just posting the idea here to gauge reaction and get @Gregory's blessing for it but I feel that as long as we're careful it could be a great way to familiarise ourselves with different regional scenes.Obviously as it's your site, I wouldn't want to do this without your permission @Gregory and if you think it's a **** idea or don't want the potential hassle then I completely understand! * Edited at 01.02.2022, 9:21 PM ET *

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: It’s my mega unpopular opinion but I think dariush has a much better chance than people are giving him. Beniel is by far the most complete fighter he’s fought and I think that’s just a fact. He’s an established vet, is still in his prime, and has fought legit competition. He’s really aggressive with his striking and hits deceptively hard. His wrestling is pretty good too and he’s gonna have a major bjj advantage over islam (mostly meaning I don’t think Islam will be able to sub him easy like he has his last three opponents). I think one of the key things in this matchup too will be his tdd. It’s a guarantee that islam will take him down/ that this fight will end up playing out mostly on the ground BUT dariush hasn’t been taken down more than 1 time in a fight and has a legit 80% tdd. I think he’s gonna be able to give islam resistance on the takedown front (I know he was able to take down arman pretty easily but dariush has faced some solid grapplers in his own right). Granted his tdd is a little flawed because he hasn’t faced a ridiculous amount of attempts but still. I am worried about beniels cardio as he’s never been 5 rounds and we have seen him gas before but still

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: And then obviously I’m going to pick him to beat islam because I am a big dariush fan
